races.race_name: Fitzdares Handicap

runners.name: Seventy
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 1331 since February, the wins at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) and Windsor (1m2f, good to firm), last time overcoming traffic issues to prevail narrowly; solid contender up just 2lb.
runners.comment: Overcame traffic issues to prevail narrowly at Windsor last time; solid chance up just 2lb

runners.name: Imperial Cult
runners.spotlight: Made all at Lingfield (1m4f) on seasonal debut, taking record at that venue to 3-6 and AW strike-rate to 3-15; only 1-21 on turf but registered the win off current mark; may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Reappearance win took AW record to 3-15; only 1-21 on turf but is on a workable mark

runners.name: Mrembo
runners.spotlight: Has several pieces of C&D form, including a success in 2023 and respectable fourth in latest start; competitively handicapped, having gained last three wins off just 1lb lower; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Has several pieces of C&D form, including respectable fourth last time; in the mix

runners.name: Opera Wave
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo whose form in Class 5 handicaps comprises a close second (1m2f) and narrow success (1m3f, AW); interesting returned to this grade with Kempton reappearance under her belt.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo whose Class 5 handicap form comprises a close second and a narrow win

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner, including over C&D, but never landed a blow (behind Mrembo) returned to this scenario last time; still has never defied a mark as high as current one, on turf.
runners.comment: Never landed a blow returned to this C&D most recently; mark remains difficult

runners.name: Free World
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo; not particularly solid on his Meydan form and he suffered a heavy defeat at York (1m4f) on debut for new stable; best watched unless the market suggests better is likely.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced; suffered a heavy defeat on stable debut last time

races.race_name: Track Radio On Digital & DAB Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Paradise Walk
runners.spotlight: Reasonably consistent thus far; finished second in her AW attempts, then fourth at Newmarket (6f, good to firm; blinkers fitted) on turf/handicap debut; solid claims.
runners.comment: Reasonably consistent thus far; fourth at Newmarket on turf/handicap debut last time

runners.name: Percy's Star
runners.spotlight: Beat all bar the market leader in similar event over C&D (good to firm) to build on his debut effort despite still seeming green; respected with another step forward plausible.
runners.comment: Beat all bar the market leader over C&D last time, despite still seeming green; respected

runners.name: Space Party
runners.spotlight: Showed clear promise with third of 14, faring best of the newcomers, in Doncaster novice contest (6f, good to firm) and has possibilities provided he makes normal progress.
runners.comment: Showed clear promise with third of 14, best of the newcomers, in Doncaster contest

runners.name: Trinculo
runners.spotlight: Well held in the Woodcote in sole 2yo start, then had gelding operation; fared better dropped in grade at Kempton (6f, AW) on reappearance, finishing third; could go well.
runners.comment: Fared better dropped in grade at Kempton on reappearance; could go well

runners.name: Moravian
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly who brings the worst form and should find handicaps more suitable shortly; well behind Percy's Star over C&D last time.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form

races.race_name: Fitzdares Telephone & Text Betting Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Mythical Valentine
runners.spotlight: Placed in 1m4f maidens at Kempton (AW) and Catterick (good) this term, beaten only by main market rival last time; solid-looking contender who is the pick of the runners with experience.
runners.comment: Placed in a couple of 1m4f maidens this term; the pick of the runners with experience

runners.name: Sea Canary
runners.spotlight: 120,000euros yearling; by Sea The Stars and first foal of unraced sister to German 1m2f Group 2 winner Tusked Wings and useful German 2m winner Tangut; represents notable connections who operate at a good strike-rate and regularly get above-average horses; respected debutante.
runners.comment: 120,000euros yearling; by Sea The Stars; notable connections; respected debutante

runners.name: Aukus
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly whose latest effort, namely 100-1 fifth with blinkers fitted at Salisbury, may not be entirely reliable as she was able to dictate the pace; either way looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Latest effort may not be entirely reliable; either way looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Treble Nine
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros foal, 60,000gns yearling; by Ghaiyyath and first foal of unraced sister to 1m3f Listed winner/Derby runner-up Khalifa Sat; stablemate of Mythical Valentine; one of two newcomers in the field; market useful.
runners.comment: 60,000gns yearling; by Ghaiyyath; one of two newcomers in the field; market useful

runners.name: Joanna Hiffernan
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who brings the worst form, having recorded a meagre RPR with her sixth-place finish at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on belated debut; perhaps one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: 4yo filly who recorded a meagre RPR in 1m3f AW event on belated debut

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Green
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly over 5f; successful three times for previous yard and beaten only half a length in both starts for new stable, while leaving the impression this return to 6f is perhaps worth exploring; in-form filly who warrants respect.
runners.comment: Beaten only half a length in both starts for new yard; in-form filly who warrants respect

runners.name: Sudden Flight
runners.spotlight: Possibly unsuited by soft ground in last two appearances, taking turf record to 0-5; has shown some promise otherwise on grass and remains well treated on AW form (mark in that sphere is 8lb higher).
runners.comment: Possibly best to forgive last two efforts; still well treated on AW form

runners.name: Trefor
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 11542 over C&D; good spell last summer featured three wins elsewhere; evidence from previous campaigns suggests he'll step up on reappearance duck egg and go well.
runners.comment: Record of 11542 over C&D; likely to step up on reappearance duck egg and go well
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Uncle Don
runners.spotlight: Turf maiden winner who is 0-11 in handicaps but went down by only a neck in Lingfield AW event (6f) on stable/seasonal debut and is worth keeping in mind despite posting a lesser effort since.
runners.comment: 0-11 in handicaps but went close on stable/seasonal debut (penultimate start)

runners.name: Justcallmepete
runners.spotlight: Nine wins on AW; scored at Bath on penultimate outing but is only 2-23 on turf and latest effort confirms that marks in the 80s are probably beyond him, at least nowadays, in this sphere.
runners.comment: Scored on penultimate start but only 2-23 on turf and current mark looks difficult

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Has recent Windsor form, having posted one of his best efforts this year when third over C&D on penultimate start; infrequent winner, however, and it's two years since his last domestic success.
runners.comment: Has recent Windsor form but he's an infrequent winner

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: Useful for previous yards; 0-4 this term for new stable but penultimate effort shows that he retains plenty of ability and today's assignment looks a touch easier than his latest one.
runners.comment: 0-4 this term for new stable but this looks easier than latest assignment
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

races.race_name: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Urban Glimpse
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who finished a good second in his first couple of handicap attempts and may yet go one better in a race of this nature; improvement possible with Goodwood reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo who may show improvement with Goodwood reappearance under his belt

runners.name: First Principle
runners.spotlight: Broadly progressive, mostly at about 1m and on AW, until posting duck eggs in last two outings; may prove resurgent back down in grade, and pedigree suggests the return to 1m2f is worth a go.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent back down in grade, and the return to 1m2f is worth a go

runners.name: Seagolazo
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who is 0-11 in handicaps and failed to improve for a visor (retained) most recently, while also failing to build on an encouraging reappearance effort; otherwise well treated on best efforts.
runners.comment: 0-11 in handicaps and failed to improve for visor (retained) last time
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Greek Order
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-8 for current yard but he is nicely treated on best form and latest effort ended a spell of duck eggs, while suggesting this step back up from 1m may prove beneficial, in keeping with 2023 achievements.
runners.comment: Nicely treated on best form and this step back up to 1m2f may prove beneficial

runners.name: Serengeti
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on his 7f/1m form for a few yards since leaving Ballydoyle; chance largely depends on the effects of new trip and first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of new trip and first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Auld Toon Loon
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a half-length success in Class 2 event over C&D (good to firm) and is only 2lb higher back here; possibilities with Chester reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Successful in C&D event on penultimate appearance and is only 2lb higher back here

runners.name: Merveilleux Lapin
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in Poland; has shown largely consistent form across the continent, including Listed third on French soil; the betting may offer further guidance on British/stable debut.
runners.comment: Formerly trained in Poland; largely consistent form across the continent; British debut

races.race_name: Barbara And Kens Diamond Wedding Anniversary Handicap

runners.name: Huscal
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner as a 2yo and good second over 7f a few times last term, shaping as if 1m is worth exploring; possibilities upped to this distance, provided he's back in top form.
runners.comment: Has looked worth a crack at this new trip; possibilities provided he's back in top form

runners.name: Talis Evolvere
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW scorer who is only 1-19 on turf; admittedly faced a stiffer task in Class 2 event returned to grass last time and this looks more suitable; mark is workable.
runners.comment: Only 1-19 on turf but this is more suitable than latest assignment

runners.name: Antipodes
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since neck success in C&D maiden (good to firm) last June; his form has substance and further improvement is plausible; interesting on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Prevailed in C&D maiden when last seen; his form has substance; interesting contender

runners.name: Asmen Warrior
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-12 and most of his form is over middle distances; however, looks a possible danger at this level provided he's suited by returning to 1m for first time since debut.
runners.comment: Possible danger at this level, provided he's suited by the change in direction trip-wise

runners.name: Indian Spirit
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage AW winner who trailed home last of 12 at Newmarket on stable/seasonal debut; best watched unless the market suggests he's likely to leave that effort behind.
runners.comment: Last of 12 at Newmarket on stable/seasonal debut; best watched

runners.name: Newfangled
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by a neck at Wolverhampton (9.5f) last time for a fifth AW win; upped in grade but receives weight all round and is a solid contender, with C&D form figures reading 1223.
runners.comment: Prevailed last time for a fifth AW win; C&D form figures read 1223; solid claims

runners.name: Skipper
runners.spotlight: Contrasting performances this term for new yard, winning at Redcar then failing to beat a rival at Newmarket; chance of rebound partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar on stable debut but finished last at Newmarket since; headgear applied

runners.name: Advancing
runners.spotlight: Has posted two duck eggs since most recent AW success and latest effort took his turf form figures to 370; not particularly solid kept to this sphere and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid kept to turf and others are preferred

runners.name: Bold Impact
runners.spotlight: 7f novice winner for previous yard; resurgent over 6f at Windsor (third start for new yard) most recently, while shaping as if this step back up in trip will suit; could go well off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Resurgent over 6f at Windsor last time; step back up in trip promises to suit

runners.name: Nikovo
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in the Victoria Cup last time but, generally speaking, is perhaps still on a difficult mark on turf; gained last three wins on Newcastle AW and has shown peak form there.
runners.comment: Not disgraced last time but is perhaps still on a difficult mark on turf

races.race_name: Happy Birthday Sveta Handicap

runners.name: Takeitorleaveit
runners.spotlight: Record of 141, all over 1m at Bath, since handicapping; one of the stronger form contenders, assuming he takes to Windsor.
runners.comment: Record of 141, all at Bath, since handicapping; one of the stronger contenders on form

runners.name: Marveling
runners.spotlight: Good fourth at Bath (1m) switched to handicap level last time and may take another step forward; one to consider.
runners.comment: Good fourth at Bath switched to handicap level last time; may take another step forward
runners.owner: John Troy And Mrs Rebecca Philipps

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: Placed twice on AW in April but failed to transfer the form back to turf last time, taking record to 0-13; the most exposed contender.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden who is the most exposed contender

runners.name: Sunshine And Roses
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has frame possibilities provided the pick of her AW form is transferred back to turf on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Has frame possibilities on the pick of her AW form

runners.name: Dora Vita
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has broadly inconsistent AW form; chance depends on the effects of switching to turf.
runners.comment: Has broadly inconsistent AW form

runners.name: Hurricane Ann
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has posted regressive results in handicaps and has enough to prove back up further in trip.
runners.comment: Regressive results in handicaps; others preferred

runners.name: Angry Ant
runners.spotlight: Not solid on her AW form for Jamie Osborne; sold for 2,500gns since last run; first-time tongue-tie and new sphere need to help.
runners.comment: Not solid on her AW form for Jamie Osborne

runners.name: Beryl's Girl
runners.spotlight: Ties in with Takeitorleaveit on Bath reappearance form (beaten only 2l; handicap debut) and may build on that effort; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Ties in with Takeitorleaveit on reappearance running and may build on that effort

runners.name: Accommodation
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has meagre AW RPRs over shorter but may improve for this new scenario on handicap debut.
runners.comment: May improve for this new scenario on handicap debut

runners.name: Mertoun
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced contender who may be capable of improvement now handicapping with Leicester reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: May improve now handicapping with Leicester reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Fans Favourite
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on 2yo form and ran below market expectations on final outing (handicap debut); makes reappearance.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid on 2yo form; makes reappearance

runners.name: My Old Mate
runners.spotlight: Something to find on bare figures but may take a step forward with Lingfield reappearance (handicap debut) under his belt.
runners.comment: May take a step forward with Lingfield reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Buckland Belle
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has failed to progress on AW; chance depends on the effects of this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of the switch to turf

runners.name: Henfield
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on her 2yo form for Daniel Steele; market may offer further guidance on stable/handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Weak claims on her 2yo form for Daniel Steele

races.race_name: Hadwins Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mighty Fleur
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in 3m Haydock handicap last month, having been hampered on run-in, and readily beat a next-time-out winner when dropped back to 2m4f for Fakenham maiden (also on good ground; first-time cheekpieces) three weeks ago; big player if coping with this even shorter trip.
runners.comment: Ready winner of 2m4f Fakenham maiden this month; drops back further in trip here

runners.name: Iamyouare
runners.spotlight: Displayed significant improvement when keeping on strongly to win Hereford handicap (2m3f, good) by 16l last month, on fifth start over hurdles, and has a good chance at the weights here; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of Hereford handicap last month; good chance at these weights

runners.name: Autumn Getaway
runners.spotlight: Safely held in both bumpers last season and struggled when a big-priced outsider for recent hurdle debut at Hexham (2m, good).
runners.comment: Struggled when a big-priced outsider for recent hurdle debut at Hexham
runners.owner: Mrs K Elliott & Mrs E J Quinlan

runners.name: Centurion's Sister
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in four consecutive maiden hurdles (1m7f-2m3f) towards the end of last year but didn't appear to be making any progress and her form isn't anything to get too excited about; returns from five-month break with improvement required.
runners.comment: Made the frame four times towards end of 2025 but needs improvement here

runners.name: Jolissaint
runners.spotlight: In front before last-fence fall on Irish point debut in April 2024 but, following a long absence, was tailed off in two maiden hurdles (2m3f/2m5f) towards the end of last season; drops in trip here.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to win on Irish point debut in 2024 but well beaten on both hurdling starts

races.race_name: Johnny & Tony Connell Memorial Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Lady Harriett
runners.spotlight: Suited by this track and has the assistance of Sean Bowen but has not been at her best since returning from a break in the autumn and would appeal more on good to soft or slower ground; 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Suited by this track but has not been firing on all cylinders since the autumn

runners.name: Secret Secret
runners.spotlight: Gained three of his five hurdling wins over C&D; would appeal more on ground slower than good but last month's low-key reappearance run ought to have teed him up for this return to Cartmel; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; would appeal more on slower ground but still considered

runners.name: Evenwood Sonofagun
runners.spotlight: Won this race in 2025 (good ground) and followed up over C&D (good to soft) two days later; suffered dip in form afterwards but shaped quite well before weakening into sixth in competitive series final at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) in March and returns to this track on an attractive mark; had another wind op last month (his fourth); good chance.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner last year and latest run hinted at possible return to form; good chance
runners.owner: Mrs D Chapman & Mrs E Quinlan

runners.name: Zuul
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden who has been absent since some underwhelming chase runs in the autumn (bled from nose on latest outing); not the most obvious answer on this return to hurdling.
runners.comment: 19-race maiden who has been absent since some underwhelming chase runs in the autumn

runners.name: Millford Hill
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed under rules but struggled in his first two handicaps (hurdle/chase; both 3m); swaps cheekpieces (worn once) for first-time visor and drops back in trip for this hurdle return; 5lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Still unexposed under rules but struggled in his first two handicaps

runners.name: Achnamara
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly in a low-grade Flat marathon last month but he ended the last jumps season with two disappointing runs (both 2m4f) and has a bit more to prove than some of these.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly on the Flat last month but has a bit to prove on this hurdle return

runners.name: Shinealight
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage mare who kept on for respectable third on recent handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good); needs to build on that performance but may still have potential.
runners.comment: Kept on for respectable third on recent handicap debut; may still have potential

runners.name: Celestial Reign
runners.spotlight: Won Sedgefield maiden (2m4f, good) just over a year ago but has looked out of sorts in handicaps over a wide range of trips this year.
runners.comment: Out of sorts in handicaps over a wide range of trips this year

runners.name: Road To Rosley
runners.spotlight: Struggled when upped in trip for C&D maiden on Saturday and has now been unplaced in all ten hurdling starts; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Struggled when upped in trip for C&D maiden on Saturday; now 0-10 over hurdles

runners.name: Masterminding
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles when trained by Maurice Barnes and absent since heavy defeat in 2m4f Newcastle maiden in December; has better chance at the weights on this stable/handicap debut but fitness/stamina taken on trust.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles for Maurice Barnes; makes stable/handicap debut after five-month break

runners.name: Imperial Imp
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground point by wide margin last month; ran well until tiring in soft-ground hunter chase at Hexham (3m) 16 days ago but is tricky to weigh up on this first hurdling start since 2021.
runners.comment: Wide-margin point winner last month; tricky to weigh up on first hurdle run since 2021

runners.name: R Bernard
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Stratford (3m2f/2m6f) in spring 2024 and well handicapped on those efforts but lost his way afterwards and has been absent since well-held fourth at Stratford 13 months ago; had wind op last month.
runners.comment: Absent since well-held fourth 13 months ago; had wind op last month

races.race_name: Johnny & Tony Connell Memorial Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Dusky Days
runners.spotlight: Made all over 2m1f here (good) last July and came second here on next two outings (2m6f/3m1f); probably needed recent reappearance run and has a big role to play if back on song.
runners.comment: In good form here last summer and probably needed recent reappearance run

runners.name: Poetry Man
runners.spotlight: Ran fairly well in 3m1f hurdle in January but was pulled up in all three subsequent chases; swaps usual cheekpieces for first-time visor; can't be supported with much confidence.
runners.comment: Ran okay on latest hurdling start but pulled up in all three chases since

runners.name: Half Pint Daisy
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but ran well in defeat at Warwick (3m1f, good) three weeks ago, albeit in what looked a modest race, and enters calculations here.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but ran well in defeat at Warwick (3m1f) three weeks ago

runners.name: Path Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Gave good account when third at Plumpton (2m4f, good) last month but wasn't in quite the same form at Fakenham three weeks ago and is now 0-14 over hurdles; 4lb out of the weights; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Placed two starts ago but not in same form last time and now 0-14 over hurdles

runners.name: Our Laura B
runners.spotlight: Comes here after two respectable 2m4f runs at Hexham and will be fine over this longer trip but has yet to add to her two wins in spring 2023 and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Comes here after two respectable 2m4f runs at Hexham but others appeal more

runners.name: Belvedere Bhoy
runners.spotlight: Showed benefit of wind op when scoring at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) last month, in his second handicap; upped in trip again here and has plenty of scope for further improvement; likely contender.
runners.comment: Came good at Carlisle (2m3f) last month and has plenty of scope for further improvement

runners.name: Rickety Gate
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on soft ground at Ayr last month but this race is much less competitive and the quicker surface ought to suit him better; made the frame on his previous three outings and has each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Latest run is not the one to judge him on, and earlier form gives him an each-way shout

runners.name: Suitably Surprised
runners.spotlight: Creditable second of 15 at Hexham (2m4f, good to soft) just under a year ago but not seen again until a heavy defeat in December, and absent for 160 days since; considered only if lively in betting.
runners.comment: Absent since heavy defeat in December and considered only if lively in betting

runners.name: No Rematch
runners.spotlight: Arrested decline when third in first-time blinkers at Perth (3m, good) last month but this 12yo remains without a win since 2023 and it's hard to tell whether the new headgear will continue to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Arrested decline when placed in new headgear last month but remains on long losing run

runners.name: Mickthesoldier
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in point in March and safely held in two 2m Hexham novice hurdles for new stable this month; ought to be suited by the move back up in trip for this handicap debut, though, and remains unexposed under rules.
runners.comment: Safely held in two 2m novice hurdles this month but improvement possible in handicaps

runners.name: The Roc Hopper
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six hurdling starts for Jackie Stephen, the latest in early 2025; changed hands for £1,400 in January and has new tongue-tie fitted for this stable debut; 1lb wrong but worth a glance in the market.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six hurdle runs; makes stable debut after 490-day absence; worth market check
runners.owner: J Emmerton, P H K Steveney & Partner

runners.name: Clonakilty
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by a wind op when fourth over fences at Sedgefield (2m5f, good; jumped right throughout) last month, only fading out of contention on run-in, but was tailed off in another chase here on Saturday.
runners.comment: Ran well over fences last month, after wind op, but was tailed off here on Saturday

races.race_name: RF Miller & Co Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Al Sayah
runners.spotlight: 5-14 over hurdles, the latest win gained at Taunton (3m, good; wore cheekpieces) last month; posted another career-best performance when finishing well from the rear for close third in valuable Haydock handicap (3m, good; first-time visor) 16 days ago and commands respect here.
runners.comment: Came from the rear for creditable third in valuable Haydock handicap this month; respected

runners.name: Imperial Data
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on soft ground this month, after a layoff, but this three-time course winner is now only 1lb higher than when holding on for narrow C&D (good) success last summer; dangerous to ignore.
runners.comment: Pulled up this month, after layoff, but now only 1lb higher than for last summer's C&D win

runners.name: Tranquil Sea
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with ready win in valuable race at Plumpton (3m1f, good) in April, and encountered traffic before keeping on for fourth at Haydock (3m, good; behind Al Sayah) 16 days ago; remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Landed good prize at Plumpton in April and encountered traffic when only fourth last time

runners.name: Roger Pol
runners.spotlight: Won three 2m7f handicaps (all on good) in early part of last season; made low-key return behind Tranquil Sea last month but that race was competitive and, with that run behind him, he may yet have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Made low-key reappearance last month but may yet resume last year's progress

runners.name: Lipa K
runners.spotlight: Best known for his heroics at Bangor but showed career-best form when winning narrowly at Warwick (3m1f, good) last month; 3lb rise demands even more from him here; usual cheekpieces left off; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Career-best form when narrow winner at Warwick last month; 3lb rise demands even more

runners.name: Musique De Fee
runners.spotlight: Started off for this stable with two laboured efforts but runner-up twice this month, more recently in C&D mares' race (good; in cheekpieces) on Saturday; will warrant consideration if she makes a quick reappearance in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Second in C&D mares' race on Saturday; swaps cheekpieces for blinkers today

runners.name: Gwennie May Star
runners.spotlight: Won twice over today's trip at Warwick for Dan Skelton last season (soft/good to soft); started off for Christian Williams with two pretty good efforts on good ground towards the end of that campaign but may find others in this line-up better handicapped.
runners.comment: Ran well on first two starts for new stable but may find others better handicapped here

runners.name: Speed Davis
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score by 4l at Bangor (2m7f, good) last month, after a break, and now bids to complete a hat-trick; evidently in good nick but recent 7lb rise demands further progress over this new trip.
runners.comment: Good winner at Bangor (2m7f) last month but recent 7lb rise demands further progress

runners.name: Kilmore Rock
runners.spotlight: Back in good form in new headgear this spring, most notably when winning at Kelso (2m6f, good) two starts ago; will be suited by this longer trip and is probably still on a workable mark; could have a say.
runners.comment: Back in good form in new headgear this spring and probably still on a workable mark

runners.name: Lord Snootie
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with two placed runs towards end of last season (2m7f/3m, good), and wasn't beaten far when eighth, behind Al Sayah, at Haydock (3m, good) this month; Sean Bowen rides here; can make presence felt.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two good efforts and wasn't beaten far on latest outing; considered

runners.name: Bittalemon
runners.spotlight: 2-8 over hurdles, with both wins gained in 2m1f novice events here (good to soft/good) last summer; brushed aside after making the running and finished about 35l behind Musique De Fee when upped to 3m1f here on Saturday.
runners.comment: Dual 2m1f course winner last summer; well beaten when upped to 3m1f here on Saturday

races.race_name: Hugh Cavendish Veterans' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Stratagem
runners.spotlight: Benefited from a patient ride when wide-margin winner of two-finisher contest at Sedgefield (3m2f, good) last month and has performed well twice since, more recently when staying-on third here (3m1f, good) on Saturday; will be fine stamina-wise if he makes a quick reappearance.
runners.comment: Stayed on for third here (3m1f) on Saturday; will have claims if turned out again

runners.name: Fairlawn Flyer
runners.spotlight: 10-28 over fences, including three 3m1f course wins; below form at Ffos Las this month, after a break, but is back on a good mark and this marathon trip will suit; must be considered.
runners.comment: Gained three of his ten chase wins here and won't fail through lack of stamina; respected

runners.name: Tommie Beau
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old and inconsistent nowadays but he capitalised upon some significant help from the handicapper when winning Norfolk National at Fakenham (3m5f, good) three weeks ago and he is also very well suited by this track.
runners.comment: Won Fakenham marathon off much-reduced mark this month and is also suited by this track

runners.name: If Not For Dylan
runners.spotlight: Thorough stayer who made all at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in February and rebounded from a lesser effort when third at Perth (3m, good) last month; has never run here before but is sure to be suited by this move back up in trip.
runners.comment: Posted sound effort when third over 3m last month and will be suited by this longer trip

runners.name: My Bobby Dazzler
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in good form, winning at Hereford (3m1f, soft) before good second at Haydock (3m1f, good); ran well (over hurdles) on only previous visit to Cartmel and has possibilities if this new trip suits; refused to line up and withdrawn at Aintree recently (8-1 at the time).
runners.comment: Ended last season in good form over 3m1f; a possible if this new trip suits

races.race_name: Molson Coors Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Coup De Coeur
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner race by 19l at Sedgefield (2m, good to soft) in February and showed better form when second in competitive races on final two starts last season (2m, good to soft/good); could still be on a good mark after latest 2lb nudge; must be considered.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner in February and showed better form in defeat on next two outings

runners.name: Jet Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Five-time chase winner on good/good to firm; returned from break with satisfactory hurdle run last month (usual cheekpieces missing) but fell at the first when back over fences at Bangor on Saturday and remains on career-high mark.
runners.comment: Ran okay over hurdles last month, after a break; fell at first fence at Bangor on Saturday

runners.name: Got Grey
runners.spotlight: Finished strongly to beat Jet Of Dreams on last summer's chasing debut but his three subsequent runs for Dan Skelton were somewhat underwhelming and he was sold for £10,000 in the autumn; makes stable debut after seven-month break; market may guide.
runners.comment: Won on last summer's chase debut for Dan Skelton; sold for £10,000 after some lesser runs

runners.name: Sir Tivo
runners.spotlight: Good-ground specialist with 11 chase wins on his CV, including 2025 edition of this race; shaped well before weakening into fourth at Bangor (2m1f, good) this month, after a layoff, and can make a bold bid from the front under Sean Bowen.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025 and can make another bold bid from the front under Sean Bowen

races.race_name: Warrior Down Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pony Soprano
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences at fifth attempt, with fairly comfortable success in first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) this month; back up 7lb for that but probably still has more to offer; makes obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Off the mark over fences in new headgear this month and probably still has more to offer

runners.name: Sean Og
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who returned from break with two low-key runs in February/March; usually knocks about in lower-grade races than this but returns to this track on a workable mark (even from 3lb out of the weights) and is versatile regarding ground.
runners.comment: Below form on both starts this year but this dual C&D winner returns here on workable mark

runners.name: Old Gregorian
runners.spotlight: Finished alone in a match race on chase debut in October; not beaten far when last of four at Kelso (2m5f, good to soft) in February but ran poorly over that C&D last month and needs a boost from the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Still unexposed over fences but ran poorly last month; needs a boost from new cheekpieces

runners.name: Pour Les Filles
runners.spotlight: Easily won four-runner contest at Ffos Las (2m3f, soft) on stable/chase debut in January but was probably fortunate that day and not much has gone to plan since; never competitive after early jumping errors over the big Aintree fences last month and arrives here with a bit to prove; cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Won four-runner race on stable/chase debut in January but not much has gone to plan since

runners.name: Arthur's Quay
runners.spotlight: Getting long in the tooth now and was well beaten on latest outing in March but has a C&D win on his CV and is well handicapped on form as recent as the autumn; cannot be ruled out.
runners.comment: Long in the tooth now but suited by this C&D and on a good mark; can't rule out

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)

runners.name: El Floridita
runners.spotlight: Promising debut when fifth of nine, beaten 3l, in a 5f maiden at Doncaster nine days ago (good ground); stable off the mark with 2yos this year and he's one to take seriously with improvement expected.
runners.comment: Promising debut at Doncaster (5f, good) nine days ago; open to improvement
runners.owner: M Scaife, S Perkins, Miss A & O Marnane

runners.name: Blake's Lass
runners.spotlight: 18,000gns yearling by A'Ali; second foal of a 6f 2yo winner (RPR 81), herself a sister to 1m 2yo Group 2 winner Polly Pot; stable not known for 2yo success but they did have a newcomer go desperately close at Wolverhampton this month.
runners.comment: 18,000gns yearling; dam a 6f 2yo winner; yard had newcomer go close on AW this month
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Bigalo
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; first foal of an unraced half-sister to 1m2f winner Dunes King from the family of 2,000 Guineas winner Galileo Gold; first 2yo runner of the year for the stable; likely best watched.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; unraced dam from the family of Galileo Gold; likely best watched

runners.name: Birkacre Brow
runners.spotlight: May 17 foal; by A'Ali; dam an unraced half-sister to some smart performers, notably Abbaye winner Mabs Cross; only just turned two but she is bred to be useful; stable 0-8 with juveniles this year.
runners.comment: Out of unraced half-sister to an Abbaye winner; yard 0-8 with 2yos in 2026;  just turned 2

runners.name: Yorkshire Dream
runners.spotlight: £28,000 yearling, 38,000gns breeze-up 2yo; Space Traveller half-brother to winners Songhai (Group-placed 6f AW; RPR 105) and Mercurial (5f 2yo/6f; 89); dam 5.7f 2yo/1m winner (69); stable not known for winning 2yo newcomers but pedigree is appealing enough to warrant a market check.
runners.comment: 38,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to two useful winners; market check advised

runners.name: Celestial Fire
runners.spotlight: Sold privately for £50,000 after the Goffs Breeze Ups last month; first foal of a maiden half-sister to Middle Park winner Supremacy; stable's 2yos have made a good start to the campaign and she's of obvious interest on paper.
runners.comment: Sold privately for £50,000 after breeze ups; dam half-sister to Group 1 sprinter; chance

runners.name: Mardy Bum
runners.spotlight: Raced freely but never in contention when last of six at Southwell (5f) on her debut 18 days ago; should do better at some point but the Southwell race doesn't appeal as strong form.
runners.comment: Never a threat on Southwell debut 18 days ago; can do better but she needs to

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV In HD Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Romanticizing
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns yearling; clear of the rest when pushing the long odds-on favourite close at Nottingham (1m, good) and could easily win this en route to some good handicaps.
runners.comment: Big effort last time and could easily win this en route to some good handicaps

runners.name: Glen Nevis
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns yearling who was gelded ahead of belated debut at Doncaster last month (7f, good to firm) in which he bumped into one with experience; that was a nice start and improvement would appear a formality.
runners.comment: Beaten by one with experience at Doncaster; improvement looks on the cards
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Venetian Lion
runners.spotlight: Well related; yard second string and weak in the betting when seventh of nine at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm; 11-1) and it could be a similar scenario here with stablemate Romanticizing holding strong claims; likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: Yard second string and weak in the betting when seventh of nine at Yarmouth

runners.name: Bright Sunlight
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to 7f AW winner Owl Island (RPR 91) and half-sister to winners Ribble Radiant (7.4f; 71) and Mixed Berries (Japanese 6f/7f); dam unraced half-sister to dual 6f Group 1 winner Harry Angel (by Dark Angel); takes on males for her first race but nicely bred and needs checking in the betting.
runners.comment: Nicely bred newcomer for Godolphin and needs checking in the betting

runners.name: Park Lane Penny
runners.spotlight: 45,000gns yearling; fifth foal; closely related to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Noche Magica (RPR 101) and half-sister to winners Veblen Good (5f/6f; 96) and Impeachd Alexander (6f; 88); dam 5f winner (90); would need to see market support behind her.
runners.comment: 45,000gns yearling; fifth foal; closely related to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Noche Magica

runners.name: Norman Invasion
runners.spotlight: Gelded since last campaign in which he was fifth behind a high-class winner at Ascot (7f) and a close third with hood fitted at Newcastle (1m, AW); headgear now removed; brings potential to his second season.
runners.comment: Shaped with considerable promise last season and has been gelded

runners.name: Invaluable Eye
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal; fourth foal; half-brother to 5f/6f 2yo winner Indispensable (RPR 82); dam unraced sister to 7f Listed winner Emmaus; gelded ahead of this belated debut.
runners.comment: 10,000gns foal; gelded ahead of this belated debut

runners.name: The Resdev Willow
runners.spotlight: 11,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Think First (1m/1m2f; RPR 98) and Sir Atlas (Australian 7f-1m); dam 7f-1m2f winner (94); one of two newcomers from a yard that usually brings them on steadily.
runners.comment: One of two newcomers from a yard that usually brings them on steadily

runners.name: Clova Royale
runners.spotlight: Down the field in 7f/1m races and was huge odds on both occasions; zero appeal here.
runners.comment: Down the field in 7f/1m races and was huge odds on both occasions; zero appeal here
runners.owner: Clova Farms And P D Niven

runners.name: Bee Farmer
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros breeze-up buy; not friendless in the betting at Carlisle last week (7f; 17-2) and it could be that soft ground didn't suit; it would be no surprise to see him leave that form behind.
runners.comment: Only 17-2 last week at Carlisle and it could be that soft ground didn't suit

races.race_name: Book For Ladies Day - 20th June Handicap

runners.name: Mayo County
runners.spotlight: Only turf success came off a 2lb higher mark at this meeting last year (7f, good to firm); has run two sound races over C&D on turf this season (behind Perfidia on first occasion, but is 4lb better off); well drawn and should go well again.
runners.comment: Won over 7f off 2lb lower at this meeting last year; two sound C&D runs in 2026; chance

runners.name: Ey Up He's A Star
runners.spotlight: Only win was off a 2lb lower mark at Newcastle last September; creditable third at Thirsk last month (7f, good) but bit disappointing last time at Doncaster (7f, good to firm).
runners.comment: AW winner off 2lb lower last September; 3rd at Thirsk last month but below par last time

runners.name: Elettaria
runners.spotlight: Fair run when fifth over C&D last time when Perfidia was second and Mayo County third; has a bit to find with that pair but wouldn't rule out completely.
runners.comment: Fair run when fifth over C&D last time but has a bit to find with a couple of these rivals

runners.name: Patontheback
runners.spotlight: Has won three of his 34 races on turf, the latest off this mark at Musselburgh last August (7f, good); should come on for his reappearance run and a possible.
runners.comment: 3-34 on turf, last win at Musselburgh in 2025; should improve on reappearance run; chance

runners.name: Due Respect
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in a first-time visor off this mark last July (good to soft, but goes on this faster ground); fair fifth over 6f here last time when visor was back on; each-way chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner in visor off this mark last July; fair 5th over 6f here latest; each-way chance

runners.name: Mecca's Symphony
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of promise in 2024 and fair third at Doncaster on first run last year (7f, good to firm) but was tailed off in that race on return to action last month following wind surgery (7f, good to firm); tongue-tie now tried; others preferred.
runners.comment: Signs of promise in 2024; tailed off after wind op on reappearance; tongue-tie tried

runners.name: Bantz
runners.spotlight: Ran a couple of fair races over this trip on fast ground for Gemma Tutty last season; below par on the AW later in the season and was bought for 4,500gns in October and then gelded; well beaten on stable debut but should improve for the run.
runners.comment: Ex-Gemma Tutty; bought 4,500gns in October and gelded; well beaten on stable debut

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: Three good runs this year, following up his good C&D second in April (good to firm, Mayo County 1.5l back in third but 4lb better off) by winning at Hamilton last time (8.5f, good; won by short head for today's rider Jake Dickson); chance off 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Three good runs this term, including C&D second and Hamilton win (8.5f); up 2lb; chance

runners.name: Martin's Brig
runners.spotlight: Winner at Wetherby in April 2025 (7f, good to firm) and some fair runs since; hasn't been in much form on the AW this year and perhaps best watched for now.
runners.comment: Wetherby win last year (7f, good to firm); some fair runs since but below par this year

runners.name: Brave Empire
runners.spotlight: Very useful juvenile/3yo for Roger Varian; hasn't progressed and plenty to prove after some modest efforts this year, including back on turf at Thirsk last time (6f, good).
runners.comment: Very useful in his younger days; nothing like as good now and plenty to prove
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Oscar's Sister
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run; has a fair record here, is suited by 7f and fast ground and is likely to come on for her reappearance run at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) earlier in the month.
runners.comment: 1-19; has her ideal conditions, goes well here and should improve on her reappearance run

races.race_name: Market Cross Jewellers Handicap

runners.name: Jack Rabbit Slims
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but with a tongue tie applied he returned to his best when second of five in a Beverley seller 13 days ago (5f, good to firm); that form isn't the easiest to assess and a 3lb rise won't help.
runners.comment: Tongue tied first time when 2nd in a Beverley seller 13 days ago; that form hard to assess

runners.name: Prima Domina
runners.spotlight: Second of nine on her Newcastle debut (6f) last October; well short of that level in two runs since but she drops to 5f for her handicap debut and remains capable of better; trainer won this race in 2021 and 2023.
runners.comment: Second on debut but well short of that level in two runs since; down to 5f for h'cap debut

runners.name: Brain Freeze
runners.spotlight: Her best 2yo run came here (6f, good to firm) and she didn't run badly back here over 6f 18 days ago; quick enough for 5f but she is up in grade and out of the handicap today.
runners.comment: Her two best runs have come here but she is up in grade and out of the weights today

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but she ran well at both Newcastle and Musselburgh (5f; met trouble) last month; took a backwards step at Nottingham 16 days ago and she needs to resume her progress.
runners.comment: 0-8 but two solid efforts last month; not so good at Nottingham last time

runners.name: Riffa Spirit
runners.spotlight: 6f AW winner for the Crisfords as a 2yo; two solid efforts for his new stable but he finished behind Fille Unique and Data Fata Secutus at Musselburgh last month; needs to pull out more to win.
runners.comment: Third behind two of today's rivals at Musselburgh last month; needs to pull out more

runners.name: Soca Star
runners.spotlight: Exposed nine-race maiden but she is yet to run a bad race over 5f and her second at Catterick last month looks a solid effort; one to take seriously off the same mark.
runners.comment: 0-9 but yet to run a poor race over 5f; latest 2nd looks solid enough; leading contender

runners.name: Fille Unique
runners.spotlight: She had regularly threatened prior to beating Data Fata Secutus in a 5f handicap at Musselburgh last month (good ground); a 3lb higher mark asks a stiffer question of her.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the 8th attempt when winning at Musselburgh last month; 3lb rise asks more

runners.name: Love Alive
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win a 5f handicap at Southwell in February on second run for Martin Dunne; less good twice since and she has now joined Jessica Macey; the form of the stable (one win since start of November) is the glaring concern.
runners.comment: 5f AW win in February for Martin Dunne; makes stable debut with yard having a cold spell

races.race_name: Racing TV Zetland Gold Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Fierce Fortitude
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who bagged some valuable course experience in winning narrowly over C&D last month; the runner-up had been jumping, but this 145,000euros yearling had a bit in hand and surely has more to give off a 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Beat a jumper over C&D last month but there's more to come from this 4yo

runners.name: Spoken Truth
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress after winning a maiden for Dermot Weld but it's been a bright start for his new connections and he only narrowly failed last time at Ripon (1m2f, good to soft); that was a Class 4 so this is tougher, but respected nonetheless.
runners.comment: Bright start for his new connections and only narrowly failed last time at Ripon

runners.name: Clouds Hill
runners.spotlight: Dominated a small field at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) and definitely improved on that form when a battling third behind classy improvers from top yards at Newbury (good); an easy lead looks unlikely here but he's a 4yo on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Front-runner; an easy lead looks unlikely here but he's a 4yo on the upgrade
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Divine Knight
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who won for Sean Woods on his belated debut at Thirsk (1m, good) last July; not blatantly well treated on the balance of his form (including 1m2f/1m1f handicaps) but now makes his debut for a top trainer having changed hands for 65,000gns; has to be of strong interest.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated but still lightly raced and has moved to a top trainer

runners.name: Rainbow Nebula
runners.spotlight: Steady progress last season, winning at 7f and 1m; flopped in the Spring Mile first time back but showed his true colours when emerging from off the pace to beat a Beckett improver over 1m here recently (4lb lower); he should stay this far.
runners.comment: Recent course winner at 1m and essentially progressive; should stay this far

runners.name: Danger Bay
runners.spotlight: Went back-to-back at Hamilton last season (1m1f, good; maiden/handicap) and unlikely that he gave his true running when a beaten favourite at York in September (1m2f); held a Cambridgeshire entry back then and he retains plenty of potential as a 4yo, especially at this trip (dam stayed well).
runners.comment: Beaten favourite at York in September but he retains plenty of potential at four

runners.name: Saint Etienne
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-24 in France and was recruited for 100,000euros last July; although no closer than about 6l in three runs for Brian Ellison, he made up eye-catching ground before flattening out in a strong 1m4f handicap at York's Dante meeting; this looks more his trip and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Useful in France and signs of running into form when fourth in a strong York handicap
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: Salam Dubawi
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m winner in France; only his second run for this yard when much too good for his rivals in a Class 4 at Hamilton (1m1f, good), having made the running; now has to deal with a 6lb rise in stronger company but appeals as the type who will win lots of races for Jim Goldie.
runners.comment: Only second run for Jim Goldie when doing it comfortably from the front at Hamiton; up 6lb

runners.name: Parlando
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner who has been operating close to his best in defeat this year but not last time at Epsom when in first-time cheekpieces; even if back on song, others are more intriguing.
runners.comment: Even if back on song after his Epsom flop, others are more intriguing

runners.name: Flying Frontier
runners.spotlight: 6yo with only 13 races behind him and ran just three times last year, winning at Sandown (1m2f, good to firm) on the second occasion; 1lb lower than that day but he does need to rebound from a desperate comeback run at Pontefract.
runners.comment: Fair mark despite his weight but has to rebound from a desperate comeback at Pontefract

races.race_name: racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Novamay
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a 6f hat-trick last July but went up 25lb for her troubles; low-key reappearance 17 days and may need another run or two before she is of renewed interest.
runners.comment: Three wins last July saw her soar up the handicap; low-key reappearance; others preferred

runners.name: Winged Messenger
runners.spotlight: String of good runs in 2025, winning three times with the last of them coming in the Ayr Bronze Cup 248 days ago (6f, soft); 4lb higher mark to contend with and may come on for this return to action.
runners.comment: Had a fine time in 2025, including his Ayr Bronze Cup win; absent 248 days; market useful

runners.name: Artagnan
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated in a 6f handicap at Chelmsford last October; three solid efforts have followed and he should appreciate returning to 6f; drying ground no problem and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: Three solid efforts since his AW win in October; one to take seriously back up to 6f

runners.name: Sugar Baby
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, two of them coming here (5f-6f); usually seen in a lower grade though and he will need to leave his return to action well behind him.
runners.comment: Two course wins last year in a lower grade; down the field on return; opposable
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Paws For Thought
runners.spotlight: Wouldn't judge him too harshly on his Chester reappearance (not well drawn) and he is slipping to an attractive mark; drying ground won't suit though.
runners.comment: Not well drawn at Chester on his reappearance; would be of more interest on slower ground

runners.name: Dicko The Legend
runners.spotlight: Beat Winged Messenger in a big-field 6f handicap at York last summer and he is 11lb better off with that rival today; went close at Southwell in April but he wasn't in the same form at Ascot 16 days ago; should be winning soon but perhaps not today.
runners.comment: 11lb better off with Winged Messenger on last year's winning York form; not at best latest
runners.owner: Mrs S Hibbert, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Bravo Zulu
runners.spotlight: His fine 2025 season was always going to be a tough act to follow and he has generally struggled since his return to action in December; should be well treated but drops to 6f for the first time in his career.
runners.comment: Prolific when first joining this yard but less to recommend him since December; trip query

runners.name: Dorney Lake
runners.spotlight: Looked to be coming to the boil when third at Southwell last month but two subsequent turf efforts have been blighted by slow starts; on a dangerous mark but he goes without any accessories and needs to get it right at the stalls.
runners.comment: On lowly mark but slow starts have blighted his two runs back on turf; no accessories now
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Wobwobwob
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, notably the 2023 Ayr Silver Cup, but two years have passed since the last victory; retained ability in a mixed 2025 campaign and he should be sharper with a run under his belt; drying ground won't be in his favour here though.
runners.comment: Retains a good chunk of ability but the drying ground will compromise his chance today

runners.name: Badri
runners.spotlight: Getting on for four years since he last contested a Class 4 handicap; in better form than his figures suggest, having nowhere to go at a crucial stage at Windsor two weeks ago, and he is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: First Class 4 run since August 2022; in better form than his figures suggest; contender

runners.name: Archduke Ferdinand
runners.spotlight: Won the corresponding event 12 months ago off a 6lb higher mark; not come close to matching that form in seven runs since but this spring's two efforts have come over 5f and this will be more suitable; 2-2 at Redcar and a full revival could be imminent.
runners.comment: Won this race last year off 6lb higher; 2-2 at Redcar; return to form could be forthcoming

runners.name: Tinto
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; best known for exploits at Thirsk more recently, including a close second there last May (5f, good); cheekpieces off for his reappearance and not a solid option, for all he has dropped to a good mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; only one bright spot in disappointing 2025 campaign; risky after 9 months off

races.race_name: Racing Again Tomorrow On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Woodleigh
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner, latest success over C&D last October (good to soft); fair second over 1m at Ripon last time; ideally needs give in the ground.
runners.comment: C&D win in October (good to soft) and fair run latest; ideally needs give in the ground

runners.name: Shifter
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner (good/soft); ran her best race for a while when 2.25l fifth of 11 at Ayr last time (1m1f, good to firm), although that form hasn't been working out.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; creditable fifth at Ayr last time but that form hasn't worked out

runners.name: Charencey
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins, latest in July 2024 (7f, soft); should improve on his reappearance fifth at Nottingham (1m, good; favourite but refused to settle); stays 1m2f; could go well in this low-grade event.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner, latest in July 2024; should improve on reappearance run; could go well

runners.name: Kameel
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; yet to win in four starts on turf, although fair fifth over 1m4f at Beverley last time (good to soft); this drop in trip should suit; visor now tried (well beaten only run in cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Dual AW winner; 0-4 on turf but fair run at Beverley last time; 1m2f suits; visor tried

runners.name: Heartened
runners.spotlight: 0-12 on turf, although solid run when third at Beverley last June (1m2f, good to firm); plenty to prove after finishing 35l fifth of six at Pontefract last time (1m2f, good).
runners.comment: Yet to win on turf; good 3rd at Beverley last June; tailed off last time; plenty to prove

runners.name: Wheres The Crumpet
runners.spotlight: Creditable third at Southwell in January (1m) but not so good since; seems to stay 1m2f and wouldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Creditable 3rd at Southwell in January; not so good since but wouldn't rule out

runners.name: Rock Armour
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden on turf, best recent run when third at Catterick in April (1m4f, good); hasn't done as well since.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf, best recent run when third at Catterick last month (1m4f); not as good since

runners.name: Prince Hector
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden on turf; tailed off on return to grass at Catterick last time (1m4f, good; cheekpieces; lost a shoe); has blinkers back on but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-14 on turf; tailed off over 1m4f at Catterick last time (lost a shoe); others stronger

runners.name: Wingstar
runners.spotlight: Ex-Johnny Murtagh/Sam England; tailed off on debut at Naas on only previous run on turf and also tailed off on AW for new stable in March; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Tailed off at Naas on debut only previous run on turf; no promise on stable debut

runners.name: Carida
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; finished last of 13 after a break at Doncaster last time (7f, good to soft) after some modest AW efforts in the winter; not sure to be suited by this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed; tailed off both turf starts, latest at Doncaster last time; stamina to prove

runners.name: Kalikapour
runners.spotlight: Ex-Seb Spencer; dual AW winner who ran a sound race when fourth in Doncaster handicap last August (1m2f, good to firm; cheekpieces first time); no headgear this time and may need the run after 278-day break.
runners.comment: Ex-Seb Spencer; dual AW winner; good fourth on penultimate start last term; may need it

runners.name: Tiberio Force
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-32 on turf, latest win at Ripon last August (1m2f, good); two fair runs over same C&D this season; a possible.
runners.comment: 2-32 on turf, latest at Ripon last August; two fair runs this time and a possible
runners.owner: Maj Green, S Croft & S Whitaker

runners.name: Mazza
runners.spotlight: Ex-Geoff Harker; modest form in three runs over 1m/1m2f, all on good to firm; may improve now going handicapping; stable debut; lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Ex-Geoff Harker; modest form so far, but may improve now handicapping; stable debut

races.race_name: Picnic At The Races Day Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Luna Celeste
runners.spotlight: Close second on debut at Chelmsford (6f, AW) last December and same outcome in first-time tongue-tie (left off today) at Southwell (6f, AW) last month; leading form claims if transferring her promise to turf.
runners.comment: Runner-up on both starts (6f, AW); leading form claims if transferring her promise to turf

runners.name: Reader
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut at Goodwood (6f, good) last October and bounced back from lesser run later that month (6f, AW) when third of four in warm little novice at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Promise on two of her three starts and she might not be far away

runners.name: Ardnamurchan
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; eighth foal; Ardad half-sister to 6f AW winner Twilighting (RPR 65); dam 5f/6f winner (82) from family her trainer has done very well with; check the betting but may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Twilighting (RPR 65); check market but may be one for further down the line

runners.name: Secret Harvest
runners.spotlight: 4,000gns yearling; sixth foal; Tasleet half-sister to winners Prisoner's Dilemma (Listed-placed 6f/7f; RPR 110) and Epic Express (6f-1m; 83); dam 1m AW 2yo winner (74); the betting may be a useful guide on debut.
runners.comment: 4,000gns yearling with potential in pedigree; the betting may be a useful guide on debut

runners.name: Mimime
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 5f AW winner (RPR 72), half-sister to 5f winner Gypsy Nation (55); the betting could be informative regarding this Soldier's Call newcomer but a watching brief may prove wisest.
runners.comment: First foal; dam 5f AW winner (RPR 72); check betting but a watching brief may prove wisest

runners.name: Whazzimo
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and outpaced prior to keeping on for fifth of 13, beaten just over 5l, on last month's debut at Southwell (6f, AW); this half-sister to nine winners is open to improvement on this second start and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Kept on for 33-1 fifth of 13 on last month's debut and open to improvement; not ruled out

runners.name: Bundok
runners.spotlight: 50-1 for both starts but she showed promise at Kempton (6f, AW) three weeks ago on her second start, when she travelled nicely and was hampered before keeping on for fifth of 11, beaten just over 8l; interesting.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Kempton on her second start and she's an interesting contender

runners.name: Sierra Sue
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 9l on both starts (1m/7f, good to soft/AW) but her pedigree is more about sprinting; she might have an each-way squeak down in trip.
runners.comment: Beaten about 9l on both starts (1m/7f); pedigree more about sprinting; each-way squeak

runners.name: Lavender Bloom
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when 6l sixth of eight on debut at Southwell (5f, AW) four weeks ago; however, after being detached at halfway she made some late headway and could be sharper for the experience; not discounted now up to 6f.
runners.comment: Late headway when 6l sixth of eight on debut (5f, AW); could be sharper for the experience

runners.name: The Wind And Sun
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners but down the field this spring on her first two starts (6f, AW) and a sizeable step forward is necessary.
runners.comment: Down the field this spring on first two starts and a sizeable step forward is necessary

runners.name: Rubistar
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling who was beaten 5l when sixth of eight on debut at Southwell (6f, AW) last November and has been absent since; however, she was very well backed into 15-8; interesting to see if her supporters return.
runners.comment: No threat on sole 2yo start but well backed; interesting to see if her supporters return

races.race_name: British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Spiritoftheblues
runners.spotlight: Despite being outpaced she ran out a comfortable winner on debut at Ayr (7f, good) last August; disappointing on sole run since, at Kempton (1m, AW) last October, but she retains potential and this sort of trip may well be right up her street; could have a big part to play.
runners.comment: Won on debut last August then disappointing in October; off since but retains potential

runners.name: Rogue Defence
runners.spotlight: Sent off 9-2 when winning on debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) four weeks ago; carries a 7lb penalty today and the extra 2f isn't an obvious plus judged on his pedigree, but this half-brother to Group 3 winner Meraas could have plenty more to offer and he's a leading player.
runners.comment: Won on debut four weeks ago (1m, AW); up in trip under penalty but still a leading player

runners.name: Snapback
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros yearling; third foal; half-brother to French winners Luna Rock (1m4f) and Neuschwanstein (9.5f); dam unraced half-sister to 1m1f Group 3 winner Aim To Please; top stable has very healthy 29% strike-rate at Leicester; could play a leading role on debut.
runners.comment: Top yard has very healthy 29% strike-rate at Leicester; could play a leading role on debut

runners.name: Relentless Hero
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability, albeit without threatening, on his two starts (7f/1m2f, AW/good to firm); improvement is necessary today but he's in good hands and may be able to take a step forward at some point; not discounted.
runners.comment: Ability on both starts; improvement needed but could take step forward at some point

runners.name: Adalo
runners.spotlight: 6l seventh of nine at 15-2 on this month's debut at Goodwood (1m, good), where he wore a tongue-tie (retained); however, his pedigree suggests the step up in trip will suit and he could be a big improver for his top yard.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests the step up in trip will suit; could be a big improver for his top yard

runners.name: Astracornus
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably when sixth of six, beaten just over 9l, in warm maiden over C&D (good to firm) last month on his second start; could take another step forward today and he has an each-way chance.
runners.comment: Respectable run in warm C&D maiden last month on second start and has an each-way chance

runners.name: Howluckyarewe
runners.spotlight: 6,000gns foal; second foal; dam 6f/7f winner (RPR 97), half-sister to German 7.5f-9.5f winner, out of 6f-1m 2yo winner; trainer does well here and a market move would be interesting, but he may be one for later on.
runners.comment: Yard does well here and a market move would be interesting, but he may be one for later on

runners.name: Tinkety Tonk
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Time Test half-brother to winners Surrey Fire (1m2f-1m5f AW; RPR 87) and Clever Relation (1m2f/1m3f; 81); dam 1m2f-2m2f winner (including Cesarewitch/Group 3); from a family his stable has done well with and he could give a good account on debut.
runners.comment: From a family his stable has done well with and he could give a good account on debut

runners.name: Cosmo Brown
runners.spotlight: Down the field at big odds this spring on first two starts (1m, good); the first-time hood could help her settle but she has lots to find.
runners.comment: Down the field at big odds this spring on first two starts (1m, good) and has lots to find

runners.name: Fabled Spirit
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; seventh foal; closely related to 9.5f-1m4f winner Don Diego De Vega (including Australian Listed), half-brother to winners Al Mudhaffar (1m AW; RPR 86) and Nuwaies (French 9.5f 2yo); dam French 1m/1m1f winner (including Listed); in good hands; market check advised on debut.
runners.comment: Likeable pedigree and in good hands; a market check is advised on debut

runners.name: Sun Lord
runners.spotlight: 11-1 when 13l third behind easy winner on last month's debut at Ripon (1m3f, good to soft); that gives him something to build on but he may be of greater interest when handicapping over longer trips.
runners.comment: Debut run gives him something to build on; may be of more interest when tackling further

runners.name: Picaro
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when fourth of eight at Southwell (1m3f, AW; 28-1) last month on his second start but beaten just over 6l and others have more substance to their claims.
runners.comment: Took a step forward when 4th at Southwell but others have more substance to their claims

runners.name: Crazy Plan
runners.spotlight: She didn't show enough on her two 2yo starts to suggest she'll be making an impact this afternoon.
runners.comment: She didn't show enough on her two 2yo starts to suggest she'll be making an impact today

races.race_name: Heather Small Plays Ladies' Day Saturday 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Rokuni
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last season's best efforts for Christine Dunnett; however, well beaten last September on final few starts for that yard and his sole win came back in 2024; might be best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate and below form last September when last seen; makes stable debut

runners.name: Grand Citadel
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 21 starts but that came in this race last year (good to soft) and she's been in good heart this spring, finishing second at Lingfield (7.6f, good) last Tuesday; solid claims.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but she won this last year and arrives in good heart; solid claims

runners.name: Ravenglass
runners.spotlight: His form can be up and down and he failed to shine at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last time; however, prior to that he was a promising fourth on his reappearance there (7f, AW), and he won off this mark at Chepstow (7f, good) last May; capable if bouncing back to form.
runners.comment: Failed to shine latest but promising reappearance previously; races off last winning mark

runners.name: Fitzmaurice
runners.spotlight: Down to a basement mark but this 11-race maiden has been struggling to get competitive this year and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Down to a basement mark but 0-11 and struggling to get competitive this year

runners.name: Melissa Honey
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 13 starts but she was a respectable fourth at Southwell (6f, AW) last month and should be fine back up to 7f and back on turf; on a career-low mark and holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: 0-13 but respectable run last time and is on a career-low mark; each-way claims

runners.name: Edergole's Gift
runners.spotlight: Short-headed at Lingfield (7f, AW) in January and runs off 6lb lower here, but below best more recently and has to turn things around.
runners.comment: Short-headed at Lingfield in January when 6lb higher but below best more recently

runners.name: Blue Point Express
runners.spotlight: Sole win (from 13 attempts) came off this mark at Chepstow (7f, soft) last September; however, unable to match that form on her next four starts last season and she returns from a break minus cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Chepstow last September but unable to match that form subsequently

runners.name: Mini Mac
runners.spotlight: His form has deteriorated since his last win last May but he took a step back in the right direction when fourth of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) nine days ago; has another career-low mark today and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction when fourth of 16 at Doncaster; has another career-low mark

runners.name: Back From Dubai
runners.spotlight: 9yo who returned to form when third at Southwell (1m, AW) last month and built on it to win at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) nine days ago; he should be fine on today's better ground and is respected up just 3lb.
runners.comment: 9yo who won at Doncaster nine days ago and is respected up just 3lb

runners.name: Havana Mojito
runners.spotlight: Disappointing end to last season but he's below last May's last winning mark and he reappeared with an encouraging fourth at Bath (1m, firm) last month; could build on that latest outing and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Below his last winning mark and he could build on last month's encouraging comeback run

runners.name: Oh So Audacious
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner for Harry Eustace, the latest at Brighton (7f, good) last August; not ruled out on her seasonal and stable debut (remains in same ownership) but she'll need to be at the very top of her game.
runners.comment: Not ruled out on seasonal/stable debut but needs to be at the very top of her game

races.race_name: Smooth Radio Ladies' Day Saturday 4th July Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Fenlander
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-16 when well beaten here 16 days ago; however, this was his first attempt at 7f and he was running well over 6f previously (AW/good to firm); might not be far away off a career-low mark back down in trip.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden but some good 6f form this season and he might not be far away

runners.name: Spirit Of Jenny
runners.spotlight: She's returned in good heart this spring, finishing second (7f) then first (6f) on Southwell AW; also effective on turf; leading claims.
runners.comment: She's returned in good heart this spring on AW & is also effective on turf; leading claims

runners.name: Looks Fantastic
runners.spotlight: Beat 13 rivals to win at Redcar (7f, good to firm) last month on his second start of the season; failed to back that up when sixth there (7f, good) last time and this is his first crack at 6f, but he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar last month; failed to back that up there last time but he's not discounted

runners.name: Skellig Isle
runners.spotlight: Came from well back to win on stable debut at Chelmsford (5f, AW) in March and bounced back from lesser run at Yarmouth with close fourth of six at Wolverhampton (6f, AW); effective on turf in the past and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut in March and close fourth of six last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: Just 1-20 on turf and below par at Lingfield (7f, good) last Tuesday, but prior to that he finished close-up twice in a row here (6f/7f, good to firm) and he can be bang there if leaving last time behind.
runners.comment: Placed twice in a row here before below-par run at Lingfield; bang there if bouncing back

runners.name: Shazani
runners.spotlight: On a handy mark on his 1m/7f form last autumn but hasn't been shaping this spring as though he's about to return to winning ways.
runners.comment: On a handy mark but not shaping this spring as though he's about to return to winning ways

runners.name: Dark Side Thunder
runners.spotlight: He has a much better strike-rate on AW than turf and was well beaten over C&D last time; however, prior to that he was second of six at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) and is not written off today.
runners.comment: Much better strike-rate on AW than turf but ran well at Yarmouth two starts ago

runners.name: Jimmy Knocker
runners.spotlight: Went close at Yarmouth (6f, good) last July and won at Newmarket (6f, good to soft) in July; absent since but he can be in the mix if fully tuned up on his return.
runners.comment: Won at Newmarket last August; absent since but in the mix if fully tuned up

races.race_name: Go West Plays Ladies' Day Saturday 4th July Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Alta Rica
runners.spotlight: Came close to a first win when involved in a tight finish on Lingfield AW (1m2f; first-time cheekpieces, now blinkered) in January; creditable show over 1m4f on this track in April but lesser effort returned to 1m2f since; reservations for win purposes.
runners.comment: Came close to a first win in January (1m2f, AW) but less convincing form since

runners.name: Rockin' The Boat
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; her 1m form on turf and the AW suggests she should find a race off her current mark but she just plugged on at one pace when a remote fourth of six on the step up to 1m2f at Doncaster (good to firm) recently; this may be easier but she needs to prove her stamina.
runners.comment: Weighted to find a race on 1m form but inconclusive regarding stamina when upped to 1m2f

runners.name: Qitaal
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins at up to about 1m2f (good to soft and soft), most recent in 2024; promising reappearance on AW in February but he hasn't done as well since and there was little in the locker at Hamilton recently.
