runners.comment: Three turf wins at up to 1m2f, the latest in 2024; not gone on from good AW comeback run

runners.name: Mythical Bird
runners.spotlight: Some promising AW runs as a 3yo and she was an emphatic winner on her reappearance at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in April, albeit the second, who has won since, was not seen to best advantage; up 8lb but has hopes of doing better again in her first turf handicap.
runners.comment: Emphatic winner on AW return (1m2f); up 8lb; may well do better; first turf handicap

runners.name: Hamadhan
runners.spotlight: Improved form on final Irish start (about 1m2f; AW) in September; always out the back at Chester (about 1m2f, good; 50-1) recently on handicap and yard debut, but he kept on well enough to think this stiffer track will suit; his mark may still be on the high side, though.
runners.comment: Never-nearer remote 7th on yard/handicap debut at Chester; this stiffer track could suit
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Diderot
runners.spotlight: AW wins at 1m and 1m2f and he's 0-8 on turf; refused to race on second run back from an absence and, following his fading fifth at Redcar (1m2f) recently, a further drop in the weights could be in order; at least this is a drop in grade, though.
runners.comment: AW wins at 1m/1m2f; 0-8 on turf and faded over 1m2f at Redcar last time; down in grade

races.race_name: For Best Ticket Deals Book Online At leicester-racecourse.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Made debut in March; improved to win 7f novice on Southwell AW in April but he flagged when attempting that trip on turf debut at Ascot 17 days ago; of some interest in first 6f run, though.
runners.comment: Debut in March; improved to win 7f AW novice in April; below best latest; first 6f run

runners.name: Do Bronxs
runners.spotlight: Won 6f debut at Ayr as 2yo but highly tried and found wanting for the rest of 2025; however, he did well to be a close fifth of 12 on his handicap debut at Chester (6f, good) this month, considering he was drawn high and raced wide for much of the way; thereabouts off the same mark.
runners.comment: Highly tried after debut win; poorly drawn but good 5th on handicap debut at Chester

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Successful 2yo debut and later close up in a 6f York handicap (good to firm) last August; best effort since when beaten about 1l at Newmarket in April but the step up to 7f back at York last time wasn't a particular positive, even though he ran perfectly well in fifth of 14; major contender back at 6f on drying ground.
runners.comment: Creditable 5th over 7f at York recently; return to 6f on drying ground should suit

runners.name: Jazl
runners.spotlight: Has good sprinting connections; useful form on Thirsk debut (6f, good to firm) and a Brighton defeat next time can be forgiven; gelded since; travelled strongly and found plenty when winning on his Windsor reappearance and while the form is hard to weigh up, with the favourite running his first moderate race, this one has potential now handicapping.
runners.comment: Good sprint connections; wide-margin winner (6f, good to firm) on return; handicap debut

runners.name: Pilu
runners.spotlight: Won first two sprint races as 2yo; no progress on final 2025 run but he returned with a useful effort at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) in April; has a little to find with First Legion on that run but the latter had had a sharpener which this one lacked; interesting for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Won first two sprint starts as 2yo; good return at Newmarket (6f, good to firm); improve

runners.name: Eternal Solace
runners.spotlight: Won two of her first three starts as a juvenile and also went close in a 6f AW handicap in November off today's mark; however, this season is proving more complicated and she was said never to have travelled over 5f at Newmarket recently; now goes in cheekpieces with work to do.
runners.comment: Won 2 of first 3 starts; has looked exposed as a 3yo; now goes in cheekpieces

runners.name: Front Line Fury
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins (both on good) last summer but unable to maintain progress and he was tried in cheekpieces last September; never involved on recent comeback at Newmarket but he may come on plenty on just his second run since wind surgery with a tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins as 2yo; never involved on first run since wind op; now tongue tied

runners.name: Amazing Journey
runners.spotlight: Both 2yo wins at 6f on good to firm; had some surface and trip queries subsequently and gelded since; lacks a recent run but conditions will suit on reappearance.
runners.comment: Both 2yo wins at 6f on good to firm; lacks a run but conditions should suit on return

runners.name: Lope El Fuego
runners.spotlight: Broke duck on second start, at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last July; lost out only narrowly in a Salisbury novice next time but had more to do when down the field in a 5f Listed race when last seen nine months ago; race readiness the query today.
runners.comment: Won on 2nd start (6f, good to firm) as 2yo; off since August; race readiness the query

runners.name: Enricher
runners.spotlight: Made progress as a 2yo, winning the latest of four attempts, over 6f on Wolverhampton AW; that was last August, though, and no blame attached if he needs the run on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Made progress as 2yo, winning over 6f on AW in August; off since; handicap debut

runners.name: Rogue Supremacy
runners.spotlight: Two wins at about 5f (good to firm and good) as 2yo for previous yard; no progress for this one in 2026, finishing in mid-division at Newmarket (first-time cheekpieces, retained) in April; gelded since; likely needs more weight off his back kept to 6f.
runners.comment: Two wins at about 5f as 2yo; no progress for new yard this year; gelded since last seen

runners.name: Inca Heights
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of 5f Newbury maiden on debut last April and showed comparable form over 6f among some testing assignments after; gelded since; withdrawn from intended reappearance this month; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Similar level of form over 5f and 6f as 2yo; withdrawn from intended return; check market

races.race_name: Racing Again Tomorrow Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Gearing's Point
runners.spotlight: All four wins at about this trip in 2023, including over C&D; not totally clear whether she's still the same mare since back from an absence but she gave Moon Over The Sea some grief at Windsor (about 1m3f, good to firm) on her recent return to turf and has another good run in her.
runners.comment: All 4 wins in 2023, including over C&D; good 2nd to Moon Over The Sea back on turf latest

runners.name: Little She
runners.spotlight: Not yet off the mark but in the frame seven times, at 6f to about 1m1f; kept on quite nicely when upped to 1m2f at Windsor recently but needs to show she has the stamina to outstay a couple of proven 1m4f performers.
runners.comment: Maiden; in the frame seven times; creditable show upped to 1m2f latest; another new trip

runners.name: Clipsham Noble
runners.spotlight: Sole win over 1m at Brighton last June; running well for new yard and although he hasn't shone over this far in two previous attempts he kept on quite well when a close fourth over 1m2f here last time; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Sole win at 1m; in fair form; kept on quite well over 1m2f here latest; stamina to prove

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: In fine form since returned to turf this spring, winning two races at about 1m3f (firm and good to firm); had a 7lb higher mark to contend with when outpointed back at Bath since but the winner wasn't badly treated on past Flat form and autumn hurdle wins; now 3lb lower; major player.
runners.comment: Two wins at about 1m3f this spring; outpointed latest but in the thick of it again

runners.name: Coverbridge
runners.spotlight: A 7f AW winner as a 2yo but just minor form in handicaps at 6f to 1m6f for this yard since April 2025; others are much higher up the list.
runners.comment: A 7f AW winner as 2yo; just minor handicap form since April 2025 for this yard

runners.name: Rose Warrior
runners.spotlight: Signs of some ability in a handful of starts for previous yard but he was beaten a long way out on his debut for this one, on Southwell AW (1m6f) in April; has worn blinkers and cheekpieces, now goes in a visor with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Signs of some ability for previous yard; beaten a long way out on debut for this one (AW)

runners.name: Abila
runners.spotlight: Has had few chances but little to show for four handicap runs on AW at 1m1f-1m6f; has dropped 13lb in that time but still not very tempting despite the possibility that the return to turf will suit.
runners.comment: Little to show for 4 handicap runs on AW; down 13lb in that time but lots more needed

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has been soundly beaten off modest marks in handicaps, including at 1m4f, for this trainer in 2026; best watched again.
runners.comment: Maiden; soundly beaten in handicaps for this trainer in 2026, including at this trip

races.race_name: Oddschecker Free Bets Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: The Wise Traveller
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner but hasn't achieved a great deal since his 25-1 Plumpton win last September; however, he likes good ground and his mark is dropping.
runners.comment: Not in the best of form but he likes good ground and his mark is dropping

runners.name: Alan Bresil
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been looking a winner waiting to happen and was behind The Wise Traveller two runs back; opposable right now.
runners.comment: Hasn't been looking a winner waiting to happen and behind The Wise Traveller two runs back

runners.name: Geordie Night
runners.spotlight: Exposed and only 1-13 over hurdles, while his latest third at Doncaster three months ago is nothing to shout about; however, as a winner off 1lb higher 12 months ago (2m7f, good) he commands respect.
runners.comment: As a winner off 1lb higher 12 months ago (2m7f, good) he commands respect

runners.name: Another Day Out
runners.spotlight: Only 3lb higher than for her narrow C&D win on good ground in March and was odds-on favourite for her return to fences 18 days ago only to depart mid-race; she's probably the one to beat; usual cheekpieces are replaced by an eyeshield.
runners.comment: Won over C&D before falling mid-race back over fences when a shade of odds-on

races.race_name: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Handicap Chase

runners.name: Slaney Opera
runners.spotlight: 3-16 in points and 2-9 over fences; two respectable runs for this yard, over 3m5f and 3m on good ground; obvious claims provided switching cheekpieces for blinkers doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: Obvious claims provided switching cheekpieces for blinkers doesn't backfire

runners.name: Cloudy Wednesday
runners.spotlight: Multiple chase winner with plenty of form here, including a runner-up finish in first-time blinkers last week over 2m4f (good); has won over this far.
runners.comment: Multiple chase winner with plenty of form here, including last week's second

runners.name: Crac De Megaudais
runners.spotlight: 2m4f hurdle winner on good ground; 0-5 as a chaser and failed to convince stamina-wise when stepped up to 3m at Southwell, finishing 28l off the winner; to give him the benefit of the doubt, that run was after a break.
runners.comment: Failed to convince stamina-wise when stepped up to 3m at Southwell

runners.name: Uncle Al
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb higher at Sedgefield during the winter (2m5f, soft) but nothing since, as the second in March was remote.
runners.comment: Has achieved very little since he won off 2lb higher at Sedgefield in December

races.race_name: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eternal Angel
runners.spotlight: Didn't last long with Nicky Henderson but showed a good bit of ability; comfortably held in three runs for an Irish yard, including 2m4f/2m6f handicaps; now with Tom Lacey and if he can get him back to the form he was showing for Henderson, then a race of this nature should be within his capabilities.
runners.comment: If tapping back into initial promise for new yard then he might well be the answer

runners.name: Sword Of Wessex
runners.spotlight: Odds-on winner over nearly 1m4f at Yarmouth (good) last month and ran okay on faster ground at Leicester (both runs in a visor); he's been found a very winnable race for his hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Makes hurdling debut on the back of competitive runs on the Flat

runners.name: Tangled Webb
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in both her runs over hurdles at huge odds; now hooded.
runners.comment: Pulled up in both her runs over hurdles at huge odds; now hooded

runners.name: Land Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Beaten 30l and 13l in two good-ground bumpers at Worcester last summer.
runners.comment: Beaten 30l and 13l in two good-ground bumpers at Worcester last summer

runners.name: Crazy About Her
runners.spotlight: Has a couple of seconds to her name but there's no real substance to her form; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has a couple of seconds to her name but there's no real substance to her form

runners.name: Palma Springs
runners.spotlight: Only sixth of the eight runners when 13-8 for a bumper here; fared no better on her switch to hurdling at Ludlow where she was 18l behind the winner; this is weak but she still has to step up; the tongue-tie may help.
runners.comment: This is weak but she still has to step up; the tongue-tie may help

races.race_name: Download The Oddschecker App Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Lady Jago
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner (2m4f-3m1f) on good ground for Dan Skelton in spring 2025; has since lost her way but the signs were more encouraging here last time (2m7f), for all that she was no match for the winner; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Well treated on old form and latest second here was a step in the right direction

runners.name: Kill The Groove
runners.spotlight: Went close in a handicap hurdle in January off 9lb higher but it's been a quiet start over fences, beaten 38l and 20l; risks attached for now.
runners.comment: Threatened over hurdles; it's been a quiet start over fences, beaten 38l and 20l

runners.name: Ballyhiho
runners.spotlight: Fairly treated on hurdle form and seems to need good ground, on which she was well beaten on chase debut two months ago; could be wiser for that experience but she was a remote last.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler but she was a remote last on her switch to fences in March

runners.name: She Won't Mind
runners.spotlight: Won a point in 2023 but unseated mid-race on chase debut a year ago and recently returned from layoff with ordinary run over hurdles (0-7); tongue-tie is now added.
runners.comment: Unseated mid-race in her previous chase and another ordinary hurdle run since

runners.name: Lady's Choice
runners.spotlight: Her fourth of five in a Downpatrick maiden hurdle last May is her standout piece of form, since when she's been tailed off and pulled up (sharply) in good-ground handicaps; goes chasing with much to prove.
runners.comment: Has looked limited over hurdles and goes chasing with much to prove

races.race_name: Oddschecker Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Knighton
runners.spotlight: Only four completed when second on his handicap debut at Ludlow (2m) but the winner was a Pauling-trained favourite; that was his first run on good ground as a jumper.
runners.comment: Beaten by a Pauling-trained favourite when runner-up on his handicap debut

runners.name: Saachi
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW (admittedly at the basement level) prior to another no-show over hurdles, in a 2m good-ground handicap at Southwell (22-1); hard to be at all confident.
runners.comment: Another poor run over hurdles last time having been running well on the AW

runners.name: Stay In The Game
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but has competitive form on the Flat and in maiden hurdles; beaten 18l when ninth of 20 in a Down Royal handicap three weeks ago, which wasn't that bad a run; this is far less competitive.
runners.comment: Beaten 18l when ninth of 20 in a Down Royal handicap three weeks ago

runners.name: Sunset In Paris
runners.spotlight: Low grade on the Flat; pulled up in a Southwell handicap two weeks ago when 80-1; now visored.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a Southwell handicap two weeks ago when 80-1; now visored

runners.name: Hellion
runners.spotlight: Twice third on the Flat for Brian Meehan; has shown nothing so far over hurdles (latterly after a wind operation) but things might pick up now handicapping in new headgear; the market could be informative.
runners.comment: Poor hurdle form thus far but things might pick up now handicapping

races.race_name: Oddschecker+ Subscription Service Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Raffles Nobu
runners.spotlight: Only win in 19 came in 2023 when based in France; had plenty of chances since and he went out like a light at Uttoxeter three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Sole win was in France in 2023 and he went out like a light at Uttoxeter three weeks ago

runners.name: Charlie My Boy
runners.spotlight: Won this last year off a 5lb lower mark and he was returning from 192 days off that day, so a break might well have done him good.
runners.comment: Won this last year off a 5lb lower mark and he was returning from 192 days off that day

runners.name: Tyson
runners.spotlight: Had been disappointing for this yard but went off odds-on at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March and duly won with ease; however, Fakenham was another backward step, even allowing for the 9lb rise.
runners.comment: Sole bright spot for this yard was the win at Newcastle; an underwhelming run since

runners.name: Below The Belt
runners.spotlight: Much improved effort on handicap debut when a close second at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) and has clearly found his level; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Improved effort to be second on his handicap debut and has clearly found his level

runners.name: The Truant
runners.spotlight: 3-36 on the Flat and 2-30 over hurdles after signing off for Paul Flynn with a Punchestown win last October (2m, good to yielding); has been running okay on the AW for this yard; not discounted.
runners.comment: Has been running okay on the AW for this yard; not discounted back over hurdles

races.race_name: McHale ProPel Hurdle

runners.name: Farren Glory
runners.spotlight: 2-10 over hurdles and rated 143; Grade 1 novice winner at Fairyhouse in December 2023 but that was his last win in this sphere; vast majority of best form is on soft and heavy ground, so could probably do with some rain; would be getting 9lb from Daddy Long Legs in a handicap; place claims.
runners.comment: Long losing run over timber, most of best form on soft-heavy, rain would boost chance

runners.name: Daddy Long Legs
runners.spotlight: Grade 3 and Listed winner over hurdles and rated 152; last sighted finishing well beaten in Southwell Flat maiden won by Constitution Hill; not seen over timber since Sussex Champion Hurdle second at Plumpton in April 2025; best treated by race conditions and the one to beat; cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: Rated 152, last two starts on Flat, best treated by race conditions, cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Blizzard Of Oz
runners.spotlight: 146-rated chaser; decent strike rate of 3-6 over hurdles; won on testing ground at Tipperary (2m7f, sft-hvy) on penultimate but pulled up in Fairyhouse Grade 2 on latest (2m4f, yld-sft); acts on better ground but this trip may be on the sharp side; place shout.
runners.comment: 146 rated chaser, 3-6 over hurdles, 2m7f win on penultimate, this trip may be too sharp

runners.name: Runcok
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler and chaser; rated 133 over timber; soundly beaten in chases at Galway and here when last seen last summer; has since left Willie Mullins; lacks a run and owners' retained rider JJ Slevin prefers Blizzard Of Oz.
runners.comment: Off since below par chase run here last August, new yard, Slevin prefers Blizzard Of Oz

runners.name: The Weighbridge
runners.spotlight: Capri gelding; placed in one of two starts in point-to-points; this looks a tough introduction to life under Rules; others preferred.
runners.comment: Capri gelding, placed in a point, this looks a tough start to life under Rules

runners.name: Thecompanysergeant
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner and winning chaser is 2-11 over hurdles and rated 132; has lost his way since fine second in the Plate at Cheltenham in 2025; pulled up in Leopardstown chase when last seen in February; revival needed.
runners.comment: 2-11 over hurdles and rated 132, pulled up when last seen in February, revival needed

runners.name: Georgie's Pearl
runners.spotlight: No show in bumpers and similar story when soundly beaten on hurdles debut in a Naas maiden when last seen in September; unlikely.
runners.comment: No show in bumpers or on hurdles debut when last seen in September, unlikely

runners.name: Eye Contact
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats at huge odds both starts over hurdles; similar story in three Flat runs; cheekpieces go on; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No impact yet over hurdles or on the Flat, cheekpieces tried, hard to fancy

races.race_name: McHale Fusion 4 Range Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: De Jour En Jour
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in both point-to-points; placed in four of five starts over hurdles and rated 117; fine second over 19.5f (yielding) in valuable auction series final hurdle at Punchestown latest; ground versatile and this longer trip should suit; big player.
runners.comment: Rated 117 and placed in four of five starts over hurdles, fine effort latest, big player

runners.name: Tinto De Verano
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in bumpers; inauspicious start over hurdles before excellent effort at Punchestown when staying on late for a 3l third (19.5f, yielding, RPR 126); leading claims upped in trip with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: Minor promise in bumpers, excellent effort at Punchestown latest, big chance with repeat

runners.name: Zambezi Moon
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of three starts in point-to-points; shock win in Cork bumper on Rules' debut in April; jumping lacked fluency at times when beaten 17.5l on hurdles debut back there 15 days ago; more needed.
runners.comment: Cork bumper winner, beaten 17.5l on hurdles debut there on latest, more needed

runners.name: Pets Georgina
runners.spotlight: Long odds and soundly beaten in bumpers at Limerick (2m3f, soft) and Kilbeggan (16.5f, gd-yld); much more needed on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Long odds and soundly beaten in both bumpers, much more needed on hurdles' bow

runners.name: Jeroboam Machin
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 bumper winner at the Dublin Racing Festival in 2024 (2m, soft); missed over two years after that win and soundly beaten on return in a Fairyhouse bumper in April; should come on for that and worth a market check on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Gr' 2 bumper winner, below best after layoff latest, worth market check on hurdles debut

runners.name: Midtown Manhattan
runners.spotlight: First reserve; half-brother to winners Major Destination (useful bumper/2m7f chase/3m hurdle) and Finding Touch (2m3f hurdle); dam bumper and 1m3f-2m Flat (RPR 106) winner; bought for E205,000 after 6l point-to-point win at Ballysteen in April 2025; resold for 210,000GBP in October as part of David Maxwell dispersal sale; hooded for rules' debut; could go well if getting a run.
runners.comment: 210,000GBP Order Of St George gelding, point winner, could go well if gets in; reserve

runners.name: Benner Wenner
runners.spotlight: Choeur Du Nord gelding; E38,000 3yo; brother to French 2m2f hurdle winner Kianna, half-brother to French 2m/2m3f hurdle winner Koregaon Park; unraced dam; yard in sparkling form; check the market.
runners.comment: Choeur Du Nord gelding, brother to French 2m2f hurdles winner, yard flying, check market

runners.name: Working Away
runners.spotlight: Point winner in 2022 subsequently bought for 330,000GBP; not seen again until November 2025 when beaten 47l in a 2m4f Cork maiden hurdle (yielding); a bit better later that month at Tramore; back from another break now; a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Off for long time after 2022 point win, beaten 11l at Tramore in Nov', a bit to prove now

runners.name: Misery Hill
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred gelding made a modest start in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle a month ago when sent off at 22-1; in good hands but can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Modest start in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle a month ago when sent off at 22-1, watch

runners.name: Commodity Broker
runners.spotlight: Homebred Policy Maker gelding; dam 2m2f hurdle winner, sister to 2m chase/2m4f hurdle winner Native House, out of bumper winner; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Homebred Policy Maker gelding, dam 2m2f hurdle winner, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Kilmond Knight
runners.spotlight: Zambezi Sun gelding pulled up in sole point-to-point in December; best watched.
runners.comment: Zambezi Sun gelding, pulled up in sole point in December, best watched

runners.name: Maison De Balzac
runners.spotlight: Experienced point-to-pointer; 1-10 in that sphere for Colin Bowe; probably up against it on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: 1-10 in points for Colin Bowe, probably up against it on Rules' debut

runners.name: Tradonthebay
runners.spotlight: Modest form on testing ground in bumpers and hurdles; much better effort on latest when backed at big odds in a 2m4f Sligo maiden hurdle, his first run on good ground; place chance again stepped up in trip.
runners.comment: Best effort yet on latest on first start on good ground, moves up in trip, place chance

runners.name: Young Doran
runners.spotlight: Homebred Kingston Hill gelding; half-brother to winners Dorans River (useful bumper/2m3f-3m hurdle), Dorans Law (3m1f chase/3m2f hurdle) and Dorans Weir (bumper); dam 2m4f/3m hurdle winner; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Kingston Hill gelding, dam 2m4f/3m hurdle winner, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Urinmydreams
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeats in two starts at Sligo, a bumper in August 2025 and a 2m2f maiden hurdle 13 days ago; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats both starts in a bumper and maiden hurdle at Sligo, safe to rule out

runners.name: Tribal
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 80-1 on debut at Killarney earlier this month and ran accordingly; 4yo may need more time.
runners.comment: 80-1 and failed to figure on debut at Killarney, may need more time

runners.name: Little Big John
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; huge odds and no show in maiden hurdles at Fairyhouse (pulled up) and Clonmel (beaten 64l); new headgear tried but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no impact in two previous maiden hurdles, new headgear tried; reserve

races.race_name: McHale Mayo Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Winning Smut
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose two-time winning hurdler; won C&D maiden hurdle last May and added a Listed handicap hurdle at the Galway festival in August; well beaten off revised mark twice since; decent run on the Flat at Killarney 13 days ago, but more needed back over timber in new headgear combination.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, added Galway win in August, beaten off revised mark twice since

runners.name: Tullyveery Lad
runners.spotlight: 5-35 under rules; three of those wins have come in chases, the latest last August at Tramore (2m6f, good); modest efforts in that sphere the last twice since return from break; no win over hurdles since August 2024 (off 9lb lower); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: No win over timber since 2024, 9lb above last winning mark, others appeal more
runners.owner: Pat Tennyson & N P Morrissey & Raymond Micheal Cody

runners.name: Fiveonefive
runners.spotlight: Gained a fifth win over hurdles at Cartmel last June but has lost his way since; heavy defeats the last twice at Naas and Aintree; is at least back down to last winning mark, but needs a revival.
runners.comment: Five-time winner over hurdles, out of form recently, on last winning mark, revival needed

runners.name: Sign From Above
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 2-14 over hurdles, the latest win coming off 5lb lower at Gowran (2m4f, soft) in October; not a bad run when fading in the closing stages at Punchestown latest (23.5f, yielding) after a break; sharper now, this shorter trip a plus and has run well here on the Flat; in the mix.
runners.comment: 2-14 over hurdles, won off 5lb lower at Gowran in Nov', trip too far latest, player

runners.name: Collaborative
runners.spotlight: 2-14 over hurdles; is 10lb above last win but has hit the frame in each of his last four runs; beaten just 2.75l at Limerick latest (21.5f, soft); new visor could eke out more, but he would prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: 2-14 over hurdles, in the frame last four starts, visor goes on, best on soft ground

runners.name: Lady Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Made most to win decisively off 11lb lower at Tramore in April (2m5f, gd-yld); beaten 7l off revised mark at Cork 15 days ago (2m3f, good); step back up in trip a plus but this is a much deeper race; big career best needed.
runners.comment: Decisive win at Tramore on penultimate, beaten 7l off revised mark latest, this is harder

runners.name: Jacovec Cavern
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win over hurdles on latest at Punchestown (19.5f, yielding); hit hard with an 11lb hike for that 11l victory and consistency has never been a strong suit, so is no banker to back that up.
runners.comment: Raised 11lb for easy Punchestown win on latest, consistency not a strong suit

runners.name: Grann's Boy
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 3-11 over hurdles; solid Flat run at Navan (1m6f, heavy) after 156-day absence in March but has failed to fire in two handicap hurdles since; remains 9lb above last winning mark; may need respite from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Good run on Flat at Navan in March, may need help from handicapper over hurdles

runners.name: Toll Stone
runners.spotlight: Sole success over hurdles a Fairyhouse maiden (2m, heavy) in February 2024; beaten a head off 3lb lower at the Galway Festival last August (2m1f, good); shaped like this trip might suit when remote third behind Jacovec Tavern at Punchestown (19.5f, yielding) in sole start since; could go well.
runners.comment: Hinted that this trip might suit at Punchestown latest, on competitive mark, could go well

runners.name: Burru
runners.spotlight: After a layoff and on first start since rejoining stable, was back to winning ways, despite not jumping fluently, in 2m4f handicap hurdle at Killarney (gd-yld); raised 6lb and now steps up in grade; will need a career best to follow up.
runners.comment: Won at Killarney latest, raised 6lb, up in grade, career best needed

runners.name: Maverick Mack
runners.spotlight: Won a Gowran maiden hurdle (2m, heavy) in November; struggled upped in trip in a Fairyhouse novice later that month; unseated early on handicap debut back there in April; a bit to prove for now.
runners.comment: Heavy ground maiden hurdle winner, unseated early on h'cap debut latest, bit to prove

runners.name: Billy Lee Swagger
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner over hurdles; won easily off 9lb lower at Fairyhouse in November (20.5f, yld-sft); ran better than the bare result off this mark at Punchestown latest (raced wide); best form on soft, however, so could do with plenty of rain.
runners.comment: Ran better than bare result at Punchestown latest, best form on softer ground than this

runners.name: Zoffman
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; won a Naas maiden hurdle (2m, gd-yld) in April 2025; has come up short in handicap hurdles since and just okay runs on the Flat on last two starts; stamina to prove for this trip and others look more convincing.
runners.comment: Naas maiden hurdles winner, no impact in handicaps, stamina to prove for this distance

runners.name: Digby
runners.spotlight: First reserve; won this last year off 9lb higher and then went close at Uttoxeter in a handicap chase the following month but has completely lost form since; yard amongst winners recently but he's hard to fancy on recent showings; new headgear combo needs to spark.
runners.comment: Last year's winner off 9lb higher, in poor form recently, new headgear tried; reserve

runners.name: Thornleigh Frank
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner for Mark Fahey in 2023; lightly raced in recent times and hasn't looked like adding to his tally in two starts for this stable; off since December and cheekpieces need to spark.
runners.comment: No win since 2023, lightly raced in recent times, off since December, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Make Good
runners.spotlight: Two wins on the Flat in 2025 but losing run over hurdles stretches back to a Grade 2 win at Cheltenham in December 2020; new yard now and has been off since October; may need this run.
runners.comment: Two Flat wins in 2025, long losing run over hurdles, new yard, may need this after break

races.race_name: McHale Tiger Roll Beginners Chase

runners.name: Scalpnagoon
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over hurdles and rated 122 in that sphere; promise in two of three chase starts and rated 120 over fences; beaten 7l over 19.5f (good) at Down Royal latest when a mistake two out proved costly; ground fine here and shorter trip a plus; big player.
runners.comment: Rated 120, bad mistake two out proved costly at Down Royal on latest, player down in trip

runners.name: Kovanis
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner; came up short in graded company and disappointed on handicap hurdle debut at Fairyhouse latest; bred for this game and no surprise to see him run a big race on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner, bred to make a chaser, big run on the cards

runners.name: Davy Crockett
runners.spotlight: Looked a hot prospect when winning his first three starts last summer but progress has stalled; heavy defeats on last four runs over timber, though testing ground probably not ideal for three of them; return to sounder surface a plus, so can't totally discount for top yard.
runners.comment: Won first three starts last summer, lost way, return to good ground a plus, can't discount
runners.owner: John P McManus & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Net Boy
runners.spotlight: 1-5 over hurdles and rated 121; bad mistake and unseated at the third on chase debut at Leopardstown in March; could go well if putting in a clear round of jumping.
runners.comment: 121 rated hurdler, unseated early after mistake on chase bow, may go well with clear round

runners.name: Sony Bill
runners.spotlight: Two wins over hurdles in France on heavy ground; yet to hit the target for this yard; beaten off 136 in handicap hurdles at Fairyhouse and Punchestown on last two starts; should make a chaser on breeding, but has to prove he acts on good ground.
runners.comment: Beaten off 136 in handicap hurdles last twice, should make a chaser, good ground a query

runners.name: Jolie Jewel
runners.spotlight: 2-18 over hurdles and rated 115; her wins have come over 2m3f and 2m4f so this trip may be on the sharp side on chase debut; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Wins over 2m3f and 2m4f over hurdles, this trip may be on the sharp side on chase debut

runners.name: Positive Energy
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 1-17 over hurdles and rated 117; heavy defeat on debut for this yard in Punchestown handicap hurdle 24 days ago; unproven at this trip; best watched in first chase.
runners.comment: 1-17 over hurdles, heavy defeat on debut for this yard latest, best watched in first chase

runners.name: Smooth Tom
runners.spotlight: Classy hurdler 4-11 in that sphere and rated 140; ground versatile; off since decent Flat run at the Curragh in October; returns with yard in form and his half-brother Royal Hollow has won for this stable over fences; could prove hard to beat if fit.
runners.comment: 140-rated hurdler, half-brother to chase winner, yard in form, hard to beat if fit

runners.name: Glen To Glen
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; won a maiden hurdle at Cork in December (2m1f, sft-hvy); has run well on this sort of ground on the level; failed to beat a rival in 9.5f handicap here on latest; not an obvious candidate for this discipline on breeding.
runners.comment: Flat and hurdles winner, last of 8 on the Flat here latest, not bred to make a chaser

runners.name: Bobbysingindablues
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and rated 100; stopped quickly when pulled up on chasing debut at Kilbeggan on first run for 267 days; watching brief advised for now.
runners.comment: 100 rated maiden hurdler, pulled up on chase debut latest, watching brief advised

runners.name: Must Go Now
runners.spotlight: 1-17 over timber and rated 109; always behind when beaten 77l on sole chase start at Leopardstown in March (21.5f, yielding); mid-field in Punchestown handicap hurdle latest; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 1-17 over hurdles and rated 109, well beaten sole previous chase start, others preferred

runners.name: Listentillitellyea
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler has plenty of experience over fences and rated 116; far from disgraced in Listed handicap chases at Fairyhouse and Punchestown last April/May before falling at Galway when last seen in August; could run well if fit after his break.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, 0-8 over fences but has run well in defeat, may go well if fit

runners.name: Arkhill
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; sole start under rules a modest fifth in a Cork maiden hunter chase in April 2025; fell on return from a break in Belcare point-to-point in March; yard form a plus but others preferred.
runners.comment: Point winner, modest fifth in maiden hunter chase sole start under Rules, others preferred

races.race_name: McHale Mayo National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)

runners.name: He's Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Didn't hang around long over hurdles and solid form in first season over fences; kept plugging away when fine fifth of 22 in deep novice handicap at Punchestown (2m4f, yielding) last month; hasn't raced beyond 2m4f but good chance if he stays (dam won hurdle over 2m4f).
runners.comment: Fine fifth of 22 in deep 2m4f novice handicap at Punchestown; good chance if he stays

runners.name: Invictus Machin
runners.spotlight: Just 1-17 over fences and that 3m1f victory at Kilbeggan was in 2024; beaten a head at Limerick last year and a neck in Listed contest at Leopardstown in March, and has crept up the ratings as a result; another decent effort at Punchestown last time when Cobden got to know him.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck in Listed contest at Leopardstown in March; Cobden got to know him last time

runners.name: Maxxum
runners.spotlight: Smart stayer over hurdles (dual Grade 2 winner); not done much chasing but returned to this discipline to win small-field contest at Clonmel and his Grade 3-fourth at Killarney was perfectly respectable; jumping will be tested here in first handicap; trainer going for four in a row in this.
runners.comment: Grade 3-fourth at Killarney was respectable; jumping will be tested in first handicap

runners.name: Midnight Our Fred
runners.spotlight: Stays a lot farther and needs good ground; some good runs in defeat around Cheltenham and when second to Perceval Legallois in 2024 Paddy Power at Leopardstown; second off much lower hurdles mark in November, but moderate form since; reckon this track will be plenty sharp for him.
runners.comment: Stays a lot farther and needs good ground; reckon this track will be plenty sharp for him
runners.owner: Mrs Florence Lockwood & Emily B Lockwood

runners.name: Ambitious Fellow
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; four-time hurdle winner (rated 130); maiden after 10 chase starts and has had jumping issues, but was second off this mark in Connacht National (3m1f) at Roscommon last summer; after a couple of spins in points, only beaten 7l in Champion Hunters Chase at Punchestown when he was a huge price.
runners.comment: Only beaten 7l in Champion Hunters Chase at Punchestown when he was a huge price; reserve

runners.name: Cobra Queen
runners.spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler; couple of wins over 2m6f last summer, but much stiffer task here after a layoff and O'Keeffe prefers yard's other runner.
runners.comment: Couple of wins over 2m6f last summer, but much stiffer task here after a layoff

runners.name: Me Wee Bonnie Lass
runners.spotlight: First reserve; three-time hurdle winner got off the mark over fences in some style when 10l winner of Listed mares' handicap at Fairyhouse; 8lb higher now.
runners.comment: Was 10l winner of Listed mares' handicap at Fairyhouse; 8lb higher now; reserve

runners.name: Lucky Lyreen
runners.spotlight: Won over 3m on handicap debut (first-time cheekpieces) at Down Royal in January, beating subsequent Ulster National winner Born Braver; in rear in the Grand National Trial at Punchestown and ordinary run in Ladies National at Fairyhouse last time; all four wins have been on soft or worse, so that's a concern; yard has won last three renewals.
runners.comment: Won over 3m on handicap debut; all four career wins have been on soft or worse

runners.name: Flash De Touzaine
runners.spotlight: Exposed but useful on his day; latest of three chase wins came off same mark in veterans' chase on this card a year ago; ran well in Midlands National at Kilbeggan next time and stays this far; after a layoff, ran okay at Wexford last month but probably vulnerable to those with more upside.
runners.comment: Latest of three chase wins came in the veterans' chase here a year ago; ok run latest
runners.owner: J Carthy & Eamon Nolan & Kieran P Ryan & Mrs Avril Doyle

runners.name: Perceval Legallois
runners.spotlight: Talented sort won Paddy Power at Leopardstown in 2024 and valuable handicap hurdle back there at last year's DRF; only 10-1 for last year's Grand National where he fell early on; well back when unseating 4 out in the National last month and pulled up at Punchestown; has worn a tongue-tie before, cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Has failed to complete in last two runnings of the Grand National; headgear combo tried

runners.name: Ol Man Dingle
runners.spotlight: Won first two chases in the autumn, including a Grade 3 at Cork; jumping left him down in novice handicap at Cheltenham; faded in the straight when behind He's Gorgeous at Punchestown and question mark over his stamina for this trip.
runners.comment: Behind He's Gorgeous at Punchestown and question mark over his stamina for this trip.

runners.name: Kings Hill
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; useful hurdler a couple of years ago; got himself a nice mark over fences and won second handicap start at Limerick (2m2f); 9-2 fav, beaten 10l when third in Ladies National at Fairyhouse (2m5f).
runners.comment: 9-2 fav, beaten 10l when third in Ladies National at Fairyhouse (2m5f); reserve

runners.name: Mousey Brown
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner over 2m4f on good ground and stays 3m; had place prospects when coming down two out in a Grade 2 handicap at Haydock last month; cheekpieces fitted then are left off, as she goes back up in trip; Slevin rides for first time and yard in fine form.
runners.comment: Had place prospects when coming down two out in a Grade 2 handicap at Haydock

runners.name: Conyers Hill
runners.spotlight: Basically a bridle horse who doesn't find much when let down, but plenty of strong form and couldn't knock his latest second to an improver at Punchestown where he pulled clear of the rest (2m4f, yielding); doubt he wants this far, but easy track will help and valuable 7lb claimed.
runners.comment: Second to an improver over 2m4f at Punchestown where he pulled clear of the rest

runners.name: Drumgill
runners.spotlight: Three times a hurdles winner here and excellent second to well-treated winner in last year's renewal; lost form after but regained it in November when winning Listed handicap at Fairyhouse; below-par since then; back from a break and, given his track record, has to be respected.
runners.comment: Second to well-treated winner in last year's renewal; below-par lately but not ruled out

runners.name: Baltic Bird
runners.spotlight: Any horse in these colours worth a second look here, and one presumes this has been the plan; useful Flat and hurdles performer and won 2m6f hurdle on this card in 2024; held in novice company last year after winning on chase debut at Down Royal; third over inadequate 2m on handicap debut at Wexford; better for that run and ground and trip to suit, albeit he's up in grade a good bit.
runners.comment: Better for Wexford third; ground and trip to suit, albeit he's up in grade a good bit

runners.name: Watch The Weather
runners.spotlight: Mixes chasing with hurdling and has run well here over flights; won a couple of handicap chases in 2024 but held in four starts over the larger obstacles since, including on first start of the year at Wexford last month; second over hurdles since and has dropped below latest winning chase mark.
runners.comment: Second over hurdles last time and has dropped below latest winning chase mark

runners.name: Fakir d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Four-time Grade 1 winner back in the day, but not the same force nowadays; has been running in cross-country chases lately, sixth at Cheltenham and second over 3m at Punchestown; trainer is a wizard with these types, but unlikely to feature all the same.
runners.comment: Running in cross-country chases lately, sixth at Cheltenham and second at Punchestown

runners.name: Native Speaker
runners.spotlight: Made all to win his maiden hurdle and chase and stays 3m1f; only beaten 5l at Galway last summer but down the field in his other handicaps; stable jockey rides but lacks a run and hard to fancy on form.
runners.comment: Only beaten 5l at Galway last summer but down the field in his other handicaps

races.race_name: McHale F5 Range Veterans Handicap Chase

runners.name: Sammy Smart
runners.spotlight: Much less exposed as a chaser than many of these and did well last season; won over this trip at Punchestown and creditable efforts last twice; likely to be handy and coming back to this trip might well be a plus.
runners.comment: Did well last season, won over this trip at Puchestown and creditable efforts last twice

runners.name: Supreme Jet
runners.spotlight: Ran consistently well when sent over fences last term, culminating in a ready win in a similar contest at Wexford where reapplied cheekpieces seemingly had a positive impact; four of these were behind him there, but he's 8lb higher now and wouldn't it to dry out too much.
runners.comment: Ready winner of similar contest at Wexford in reapplied cheekpieces; 8lb higher

runners.name: Tullybeg
runners.spotlight: Won the big race on this card three years ago, but handicapper had him after that and well held in this last year; recent form in points/hunter chases doesn't look good enough, but has been dropped sharply in the ratings.
runners.comment: Won the big race on this card three years ago; recent form doesn't look good enough

runners.name: Clonbury Bridge
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of Fairyhouse chases last year (2m5f/3m) and handles goodish ground; last month's hurdling fourth probably a prep for this; can race close to the pace.
runners.comment: Won a couple of Fairyhouse chases last year (2m5f/3m) and handles goodish ground

runners.name: Dont Go Yet
runners.spotlight: Had some good form last spring and same again this time around; hurdles winner in March, third to Supreme Jet at Wexford and good third of 20 to exciting stablemate in 2m handicap at Punchestown festival; doesn't win often, though.
runners.comment: Third to Supreme Jet at Wexford and good third of 20 at Punchestown; infrequent winner

runners.name: Happy Jacky
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner over hurdles (rated 144); did okay over fences last year and was fair third over 3m2f on his return to action at Killarney; stays 3m well, so this trip looks too short, plus he was disappointing when favourite for last year's Mayo National.
runners.comment: Stays 3m well so trip looks too short, and disappointed in last year's Mayo National

runners.name: Magnor Glory
runners.spotlight: Capable handicap hurdler back in the day and has won over hurdles and fences here; couldn't cut it in novice company after chase debut win here in 2024 (after longer layoff); nice run on the Flat when last seen in August and, given his track record, this race may have been the plan for a while.
runners.comment: Has won over hurdles and fences here, latter victory after longer layoff than this one

runners.name: Krabat
runners.spotlight: Pair of Limerick wins last season and fair fourth to Supreme Jet at Wexford on latest chase start; well beaten in last year's renewal and best on testing ground.
runners.comment: Fourth to Supreme Jet at Wexford; well beaten in last year's renewal and prefers it softer

runners.name: Grange Walk
runners.spotlight: Won a hurdle at Limerick (fair fourth in corresponding contest latest) before finishing fourth in this race last year; over three years since latest chase win and struggling in this discipline lately, despite declining mark.
runners.comment: Fourth in this race last year; over three years since latest chase win

runners.name: Dreal Deal
runners.spotlight: Bled in this last year; good form in first two starts for this yard at Killarney in the summer and creditable third of 17 in valuable 2m4f veterans' contest at Haydock last month; chance on that form.
runners.comment: Creditable third of 17 in valuable veterans' contest at Haydock; chance on that form.
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Philip Smith & Nigel O'Hare & P J Rothwell

runners.name: Farmers Lodge
runners.spotlight: Only had 10 starts, four of them over fences; after a year off, was 20l behind Supreme Jet at Wexford; capable type, though, and chase mark is 7lb lower than hurdles perch, so not dismissed.
runners.comment: After a year off, was 20l behind Supreme Jet at Wexford; capable type is not dismissed.

runners.name: Jetoile
runners.spotlight: Smart performer in Britain back in the day; went point-to-pointing over the winter and held in hunter chases lately; unlikely.
runners.comment: Smart performer in Britain back in the day but held in hunter chases lately; unlikely.

runners.name: Tuckmill
runners.spotlight: Best form is at 2m/2m1f; three hurdle wins and one victory over fences; last worthwhile form was in late 2024; well held in two starts this year.
runners.comment: Best form is at 2m/2m1f;  last worthwhile form was in late 2024; well held lately

runners.name: Fighting Fit
runners.spotlight: Long time winless; showed a spark over hurdles in October; mark was 144 after he finished second to Solness in late 2023; no worthwhile chase form since and handicapper has now relented; still, big revival needed under Cobden.
runners.comment: Long time winless; ran well over hurdles at Galway in October but has shown little since

races.race_name: McHale Orbital INH Flat Race

runners.name: A Stoirin
runners.spotlight: Harzand homebred is half-sister to winners Painless Potter (1m Flat, RPR 93; 2m4f hurdle) and Wardens Whisper (2m1f hurdle); 10-3 for Cork debut, ran well when beaten only 2.5l by a Mullins-trained newcomer; second and fourth had already shown ability too, so it looks pretty strong form.
runners.comment: Was 10-3 for Cork debut, ran well when beaten only 2.5l by a Mullins-trained newcomer

runners.name: Noble Rocco
runners.spotlight: Shirocco filly; dam unplaced points/bumper, closely related to bumper/2m3f hurdle winner Botani, half-sister to useful bumper and 2m-2m7f hurdle/chase winner Vina Ardanza, out of unraced half-sister to Elliott's smart staying jumper Lord Scoundrel.
runners.comment: Shirocco filly is from family of yard's smart staying jumper Lord Scoundrel

runners.name: Mourne Magic
runners.spotlight: Court Cave filly out of unraced half-sister to bumper and 2m4f-4m hurdle/chase winner Leac An Scail; of interest if there's money for her.
runners.comment: Court Cave filly out of unraced half-sister to bumper and 2m4f-4m hurdle/chase winner

runners.name: River Moy
runners.spotlight: E3,000 F (E5,000 vendor 3yo); fourth foal; closely related to 2m hurdle winner Crying Lightning; dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Guitar Pete (2m-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Somethingwonderful (bumper/2m hurdle); young rider won a bumper on Friday.
runners.comment: Maxios newcomer is closely related to 2m hurdle winner Crying Lightning

runners.name: An Capaill Oir
runners.spotlight: Affinisea gelding is first foal of modest racemare, half-sister to useful winners G K Chesterton (1m/8.5f) and Submariner (1m1f-1m4f), out of 10.5f Group 3 winner; the 'Shark' rarely wins bumpers.
runners.comment: Affinisea gelding is first foal of mare unplaced in points, bumper and over hurdles

runners.name: Bacharach
runners.spotlight: Cost E200,000 as 3yo; half-brother to winners Santini (top-class 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase), Dusky Legend (bumper/useful 2m hurdle) and Rockpoint (useful 3m hurdle); dam 1m4f Flat/2m hurdle winner; weakened tamely in valuable Punchestown bumper last month, but was only 8-1 for that; tongue-tie might help.
runners.comment: Well-bred and was only 8-1 for valuable Punchestown bumper where weakened tamely

runners.name: Familiar Game
runners.spotlight: Cost only 2,600gns as 3yo; second foal; half-brother to Greek/Polish 5f/6f winner Mollys Pride; dam 6f/7f maiden (RPR 68), half-sister to 5f winner Rat Catcher, out of 6f winner; yard 11-94 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Greek/Polish 5f/6f winner; yard 11-94 in bumpers last five seasons

runners.name: Getaway Harry
runners.spotlight: (E12,000 vendor F); first foal; dam unraced sister to 2m/2m1f chase winner Doyouknowwhatimean, half-sister to 2m hurdle/chase winner Fabrique En France, out of 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner from family of Champion Hurdle winner Hors La Loi III; small yard is respected and wins bumpers from time to time; Gleeson rode him when he was third in a good-ground point last month, and the winner made 500K four days later.
runners.comment: Third in good-ground point last month; small yard does win bumpers from time to time

runners.name: Telegram Sam
runners.spotlight: E11,000 F, E15,500 Y; fourth foal; dam unraced sister to smart 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Tumbling Dice, out of unraced half-sister to top-class chaser Remittance Man; pulled up in a point on testing ground in March
runners.comment: Pulled up in a point on testing ground in March; family of Remittance Man

runners.name: Time For Talking
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to small yard's recent 100-1 first-time-out hurdles winner Local Lingo and point/2m5f chase winner Hollywood Harmon; dam unraced half-sister to winners Bob Lingo (useful bumper and 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Talk The Lingo (2m2f-3m hurdle/chase), out of half-sister to useful 2m-2m5f chase winner Dev.
runners.comment: Half-brother to small yard's recent 100-1 first-time-out hurdles winner Local Lingo

runners.name: Zack De La Rocha
runners.spotlight: Third foal of point winner who is a sister to winners Just For Joe (bumper/2m4f hurdle), Naughty Molly (2m5f chase) and On His Toes (bumper); yard wins plenty of bumpers.
runners.comment: Third foal of point winner who is a sister to jumps and bumper winners

runners.name: Catofthecanals
runners.spotlight: Led but weakened both starts and yet to beat a rival; headgear fitted but yard's other runner of more interest.
runners.comment: Led but weakened both starts and yet to beat a rival; headgear.

races.race_name: Shoemaker Mile Stakes (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Formidable Man
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs William K Warren Jr

runners.name: King Of Gosford
runners.owner: Benowitz Family Trust, CYBT Et Al

runners.name: Mi Hermano Ramon
runners.owner: Red Baron's Barn LLC & Rancho Temescal LLC

runners.name: Almendares
runners.owner: CYBT, McLean Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Genius Jimmy
runners.owner: Pappas Horse Racing Corp, Colin Gala Et Al

runners.name: Twirling Point
runners.owner: Tracy D Henline & Paula P Henline

runners.name: Seal Team
runners.owner: Perry R Bass II & Ramona S Bass

races.race_name: Gamely Stakes (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)

runners.name: Thought Process
runners.spotlight: Came out on top by 2l three months ago in an allowance Grade 2; she has a shout.
runners.comment: Came out on top by 2l three months ago in an allowance Grade 2; she has a shout.
runners.owner: Little Red Feather Racing, Madaket Stables & Est B C Jones

runners.name: Take A Breath
runners.spotlight: Bids for hat-trick and successful five times from 14 career runs; narrow scorer in an allowance Grade 3 dropped down to 1m beating May Day Ready and A Thousand Miles four weeks ago; must be high on list.
runners.comment: She shouldn't be far away; scored narrowly last month.
runners.owner: Chivalry T'Bred Racing & Rancho Temescal T'Bred Partners

runners.name: Public Assembly
runners.spotlight: Fourth, beaten 1 1/2l by Take A Breath in an allowance Grade 3 in March upped to 1m2f; a previous winner here; has been consistent of late; down in trip here.
runners.comment: Fair effort in March; possesses winning form at this track.
runners.owner: Abbondanza Racing LLC, McNulty Th'breds & Medallion Racing

runners.name: Rashmi
runners.spotlight: Got the job done by just under 3l in an allowance Grade 3 two months ago; record shows she knows where the winning post is (wins-runs career record of 5-10); up in distance by an extra 1f here.
runners.comment: Won by just under 3l two months ago; has managed five wins from ten career runs.

runners.name: May Day Ready
runners.spotlight: Behind Take A Breath over 1m here (allowance Grade 3) when beaten half a length last month on debut for this trainer; proven that she knows how to get her head in front (seeking a fifth career win); cannot be ignored.
runners.comment: Looks likely to go well; beaten narrowly four weeks ago.

runners.name: A Thousand Miles
runners.spotlight: Record shows she knows where the winning post is (wins-runs career record of 5-18); finished fifth beaten just under 2l over 1m here (allowance Grade 3) last month; has to be respected.
runners.comment: Can give it a good shot; narrowly beaten four weeks ago.

runners.name: Vronti
runners.spotlight: Landed a narrow success in an allowance race (Keeneland) last month; on a hat-trick.
runners.comment: Landed a narrow success in an allowance race (Keeneland) last month; on a hat-trick.

runners.name: Hang The Moon
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in an allowance listed (Gulfstream Park) two months ago; a previous winner here; form of the stable is a plus.
runners.comment: Just touched off two months ago; has winning form at this track.

races.race_name: Coolmore City Of Troy German 2000 Guineas (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Title Role
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
